Noem focused heavily on immigration enforcement, reallocating DHS resources away from areas like counterterrorism, disaster relief, and cybersecurity to prioritize operations in sanctuary cities. While this shift did achieve a reduction in illegal border crossings, it sparked widespread protests and incidents, including the controversial deaths of two American citizens during immigration enforcement actions in Minneapolis. In addition to operational criticism, Noem’s $220 million advertising campaign, which featured herself prominently and involved contracts awarded to the husband of a former DHS spokesperson, became a central point of scrutiny by Congress. Despite her abrupt dismissal, Noem maintained composure at the Major Cities Conference in Nashville, addressing law enforcement leaders and reaffirming her co

item.title: ProtonMail Provides User Information to FBI Leading to Arrest of Atlanta Activist
item.description: ProtonMail, a Switzerland-based email provider known for offering secure and private communications, assisted the FBI in identifying and capturing a political activist involved in the Atlanta Forest protests, also called “Stop Cop City.” This activist, linked to alleged acts of arson, vandalism, and doxing, was using the ProtonMail address defendtheatlantaforest@protonmail.com. The FBI requested the information through the Mutual Legal Assistance Treaty (MLAT) with Switzerland. ProtonMail provided details linked to the payment method used for the account, which ultimately led investigators to the individual behind the account. The arrest highlights the limits of privacy even with services marketed as secure, though ProtonMail notes that payments via cryptocurrency or cash can enhance anonymity. The DTAF movement began in 2023 opposing the police plan to clear a forest for new training facilities. This case underscores the intersection of digital privacy, law enforcement, and political

item.title: ALMA Maps Cold Gas Filaments in the Milky Way’s Central Region
item.description: Astronomers using the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) have created the most detailed image yet of the Milky Way's central region, known as the Central Molecular Zone (CMZ). Spanning over 650 light-years, the image reveals a dense network of cold gas filaments surrounding the galaxy’s supermassive black hole. This cold gas serves as the raw material for star formation, and the survey, named ALMA CMZ Exploration Survey (ACES), provides unprecedented precision in mapping these structures. The data shows gas formations of varying scales, from massive clouds to smaller clumps near individual stars, as well as a surprisingly complex chemical environment including simple molecules like silicon monoxide and more complex organic compounds such as methanol and acetone. The CMZ hosts some of the Milky Way's most massive stars, many of which are short-lived and end in powerful supernovae or hypernovae. Researchers hope these observations will clarify how stars form in extreme e

item.title: Pakistani Man Convicted for Attempted Assassination Plot Against US Leaders Allegedly Linked to Iran
item.description: Asif Merchant, a 47-year-old Pakistani man, was convicted in a Brooklyn court for plotting to assassinate former U.S. President Donald Trump and other American political figures. Merchant admitted involvement with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, claiming he participated to protect his family in Tehran. Prosecutors said the plot was connected to revenge for the 2020 killing of Iranian military commander Qassem Soleimani. The plan allegedly targeted Trump, President Joe Biden, and 2024 Republican presidential candidate Nikki Haley. Merchant attempted to recruit individuals within the United States, but the plot was uncovered before any attack could occur. A person he contacted in April 2024 reported him to authorities and became a confidential informant, leading to Merchant's arrest. The U.S. Department of Justice stated Merchant was convicted of 'murder for hire and attempting to commit an act of terrorism transcending national boundaries' at the direction of Iranian official

item.title: X Tests Contextual Ad Format Placing Product Recommendations Under Relevant User Posts
item.description: X, the social media platform formerly known as Twitter, is currently testing a novel advertising approach that places product recommendations directly beneath user posts mentioning a company or its offerings. The test was first noticed in Europe, where a post praising Starlink's satellite internet service in Portugal triggered a 'Get Starlink' prompt below it, linking to the official Starlink website. X's head of product, Nikita Bier, confirmed the experiment and described it as an effort to create 'an ad product that isn’t an ad,' emphasizing a desire for trustworthy, non-deceptive recommendations. He rejected ideas like allowing affiliate links in the slot, noting concerns that it could lead to misinformation. Currently, the ad appears only in select markets, while others see a placeholder box that might display unrelated X content instead. This development comes amid X's broader push to attract more advertisers and creators to the platform, following recent introductions like 'Paid

item.title: Vulnerability of Middle East Desalination Plants to Oil Spills
item.description: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly critical as they provide the majority of drinking water in countries like Kuwait, Oman, and Saudi Arabia, with some plants covering up to 90% of a city's water needs. The proximity of these facilities to the sea and their dependence on electricity make them vulnerable to disruptions from oil spills or other industrial accidents. Recent concerns arose following a potential incident at the Fujairah complex in the UAE, which houses a power plant supporting a major desalination facility, although no fuel leak occurred. The threat of contamination from oil spills has historical precedent, notably during the First Gulf War, prompting improvements in treatment techniques. European companies like Veolia and Suez are heavily involved in the sector, with projects delivering millions of cubic meters of potable water daily and supplying hundreds of thousands of homes. Despite technological advances, the region remains highly dependent on desa

item.title: Reports Suggest Russia Shares Military Intelligence with Iran on U.S. Targets in the Middle East
item.description: According to reports from the Washington Post, Russia is allegedly sharing intelligence with Iran regarding the location of U.S. military assets in the Middle East, including ships and aircraft. U.S. officials describe this as a broad operational effort by Moscow, marking a potential indirect involvement in regional conflicts. Earlier this year, Russia and Iran signed a strategic partnership agreement, strengthening military cooperation between the two nations. Despite these reports, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth downplayed Russia's role, stating that it is not a significant factor in the ongoing conflict. Iran recently destroyed a U.S. advanced radar system in Jordan, and similar radar facilities in the UAE were reportedly targeted in separate attacks. The loss of these systems has reportedly limited the U.S. military’s early warning capabilities in the region, reducing reaction time against Iranian missile attacks. Videos from Israel suggest that 90% of Iranian missiles are currently h

item.title: Four Dead and Multiple Injuries Reported After Tornadoes Strike Southern Michigan
item.description: Severe storms and at least one confirmed tornado caused widespread devastation in southern Michigan on Friday, March 6, 2026, resulting in four fatalities and numerous injuries. The National Weather Service issued tornado warnings as powerful weather systems moved through the region. In the Union Lake area near Union City in Branch County, an apparent tornado killed three people and injured 12 others, with reports of homes destroyed, roofs torn off buildings, debris scattered, trees uprooted, and vehicles damaged. Videos from residents and social media captured dramatic scenes of whirlwinds ripping through communities, including in nearby Three Rivers. About 50 miles southwest in Cass County, officials confirmed one additional death and several injuries from another tornado. Hundreds of residents were left without power as the storms battered infrastructure. Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer activated the State Emergency Operations Center to monitor the situation and coordinate respon

item.title: Impact of Middle East Conflicts on Global Oil Prices and Economic Stability
item.description: The recent conflict in the Middle East has exposed vulnerabilities in the global economy, particularly in relation to hydrocarbon supply. The International Monetary Fund's director, Kristalina Georgieva, expressed hope for a swift end to the war. Economists warn that rising oil and gas prices passing through the Strait of Hormuz will inevitably trigger inflation. A $30 increase per barrel could raise consumer prices by 2% in the Eurozone and 2.5% in the United States, reducing economic activity by 0.8 and 1.4 points respectively. While historical patterns suggest central banks do not typically react aggressively to oil crises, sustained price increases above $100 per barrel could prompt significant monetary policy adjustments. Additionally, higher long-term interest rates could further strain public finances in Europe and the U.S., where deficits may reach up to 10% of GDP. The ripple effects could devalue the U.S. dollar and impact global financial markets, showing how economic crises

item.title: US Corporate Media's Selective Empathy for Iranians in Coverage of Strikes on Iran
item.description: On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and targeting nuclear, missile, and security sites, in what was presented as addressing threats but criticized as deliberate provocation amid stalled nuclear negotiations. The article, an opinion piece by Belén Fernández republished on a nuclear issues blog, argues that US corporate media outlets like The New York Times and Washington Post exhibit hypocritical concern for Iranian civilians. They frequently highlight Iranian government oppression—such as crackdowns on protesters, women's rights abuses, and alleged killings—to portray Iranians as victims of their own regime, thereby justifying or rationalizing US/Israeli attacks. However, these same outlets minimize or treat as footnotes the civilian casualties from the strikes themselves, including the deaths of at least 175 people (many children) in a missile hit on a girls' elementary school in Mina

item.title: California Congressman Darrell Issa Announces Retirement, Impacting Republican Hold on House
item.description: California Republican Representative Darrell Issa has announced his retirement, just three months after stating he would not step down, citing the challenges of reelection in a newly redrawn district. Issa, a former chair of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee and one of Congress's wealthiest members, had been a prominent critic of former President Barack Obama and Secretary of State Hillary Clinton. His decision creates uncertainty in Southern California’s 48th District, now favoring Democrats after recent redistricting. Issa had previously considered running in Texas but chose to remain in California, affirming his commitment to his district. The new congressional map, led by Democratic Governor Gavin Newsom, is designed to flip up to five Republican-held seats to Democrats, countering efforts by former President Donald Trump to gain seats in Texas. Issa endorsed San Diego County Supervisor James Desmond as his successor. The retirement could complicate Republican eff

item.title: US and Israel Conduct Strikes in Iran as Middle East Conflict Escalates
item.description: The conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran has entered its eighth day, with escalating military actions across the Middle East. Israel launched a broad-scale wave of strikes on Tehran, including areas near Mehrabad Airport, resulting in fires and damage to key infrastructure. The US military has targeted over 3,000 sites in Iran since the conflict began. Iranian state media reports more than 1,230 deaths in Iran, while Israeli strikes in Lebanon have caused over 200 fatalities and displaced nearly half a million people. Gulf states, including Saudi Arabia and the UAE, continue to defend against drone and missile attacks. The war has also impacted civilian life, with disrupted transportation, internet blackouts, and the evacuation of travelers through newly established air corridors. Oil markets are affected, with gas prices spiking amid concerns over supply stability. President Trump has demanded unconditional surrender from Iran, while the UN urges diplomatic solutions t

item.title: Robinhood Ventures Fund Raises $658 Million, Faces Challenges in NYSE Launch
item.description: Robinhood has launched its first startup investment fund, Robinhood Ventures Fund I, aiming to give retail investors access to high-profile private companies. The fund includes stakes in Databricks, Stripe, Mercor, Oura, Ramp, Airwallex, Revolut, and Boom. Despite an ambitious $1 billion target, the fund raised $658.4 million, potentially reaching $705.7 million if underwriters exercise their full allotment. Shares were priced at $25 but closed at $21 on their first day of trading, marking a 16% decline. This contrasts sharply with Destiny Tech100, a publicly traded fund with stakes in companies like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Discord, which saw strong investor demand and premium trading above its net asset value. Analysts suggest Robinhood’s lower demand stems from the fund’s lack of exposure to the most hyped private companies expected to go public at high valuations. Robinhood plans to expand the fund to include 15-20 late-stage growth companies and is exploring opportunities to gain acces

item.title: Milan Cortina Winter Paralympics Open Amid Controversy Over Russian Flag Return and Boycotts
item.description: The Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Paralympics officially opened on March 6 in Verona, Italy, at the historic Arena di Verona, marking the 50th anniversary of the Winter Paralympic Games. The event featured a nations' parade, but was overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, including the return of the Russian flag for the first time since the 2014 Sochi Games. Four Russian athletes participated under their national flag and in bright red uniforms, drawing some boos from the crowd. This signaled a potential full return to Olympic events by 2028. Russia's national anthem could now be played for gold medalists, a first since the 2022 Ukraine invasion. Several countries boycotted the ceremony in protest: Ukraine (whose athletes were absent despite applause during the announcement), Czech Republic, Estonia, Finland, Latvia, Poland, and Lithuania. Belarus, Russia's ally, also returned with two athletes. An Iranian Para cross-country skier withdrew due to travel issues amid escalating Middle East co

item.title: Experts Warn Smart Home Devices Are Vulnerable to Hacking Due to Poor Security Practices
item.description: As homes become smarter with Wi-Fi-connected TVs, speakers, thermostats, lights, and voice assistants controlled via apps, they introduce new cybersecurity risks. While such hacks remain uncommon, experts say they're entirely feasible because many inexpensive smart devices prioritize ease of use over security. Consumers often leave default passwords unchanged or fail to apply firmware updates, making devices easy targets for automated internet scans by attackers. Cybersecurity specialist Dr. Manny Niri from Oxford Brookes University notes that these vulnerabilities allow silent intrusions, like viewing camera feeds unnoticed, as seen in a recent South Korean case where over 120,000 home and business cameras were compromised, leading to arrests. Ethical hacker Glenn Wilkinson compares device security to padlocks, urging better 'locks' through stronger habits. Hackers typically exploit either the devices themselves—small computers with hackable software—or associated cloud services, apps

item.title: 6-Year-Old Deaf Boy and Family Deported to Colombia, Raising Concerns About Medical Care
item.description: A 6-year-old deaf boy from Hayward, California, was deported to Colombia along with his mother and sibling, according to his family attorney, Nikolas De Bremaeker. The attorney claims that U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) intentionally withheld the family's location, preventing legal interventions that might have halted the deportation. The boy, who attends the California School for the Deaf in Fremont, reportedly had no access to his essential hearing devices during detention and after deportation. The mother, Lesly Rodriguez Gutierrez, 28, had a removal order but may have been eligible for other forms of relief. The Department of Homeland Security stated that Rodriguez Gutierrez received due process and chose to be deported with her children. Advocates and state officials, including California Superintendent Tony Thurmond, have criticized ICE’s handling of the case, highlighting the humanitarian and educational impacts on the child. This incident comes amid broader conc

item.title: Iran Faces Widespread Internet Shutdown Amid Conflict and Infrastructure Damage
item.description: Since late February 2026, Iran has experienced a nearly complete internet blackout affecting approximately 90 million people, following U.S. and Israeli airstrikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and damaged key telecommunications infrastructure. The Iranian government’s National Information Network (NIN) remains operational, allowing limited domestic connectivity while the general public is mostly cut off from the global internet. Historically, Iranians have circumvented partial shutdowns using VPNs and peer-to-peer platforms like Conduit, but total blackouts prevent most workarounds. Wealthy elites, the government, and some Starlink-enabled gateways retain access, though even these are vulnerable to damage from ongoing airstrikes. The shutdown exacerbates challenges for communication, news dissemination, and evidence collection during the conflict. Meanwhile, regime-linked channels on Telegram remain active, promoting state narratives and selective information. The blackout h

item.title: MPs report wide differences in water companies’ use of bailiffs for debt collection
item.description: New data from a committee of MPs reveals significant variations in how water companies in England and Wales use bailiffs to recover unpaid bills. While some companies, such as Wessex Water, have not employed bailiffs at all in the last decade, others, including Southern Water, have used them thousands of times annually, peaking at over 15,000 instances in 2019. Yorkshire Water, United Utilities, and Severn Trent are also among the companies with frequent bailiff enforcement. The committee’s findings indicate that enforcement peaked in 2023 and 2024, following the COVID-19 pandemic, raising questions about the reasons behind this surge. Water companies claim that bailiffs are a last resort, typically used only when customers are able to pay but choose not to. Vulnerable groups, including the elderly, ill, or those on means-tested benefits, are usually exempt from enforcement, although practices vary between companies. MPs have urged water providers to review their approaches to debt col

item.title: Indonesia to Restrict Social Media Access for Children Under 16
item.description: Indonesia, the world's fourth most populous country, will implement a ban on social media access for children under 16, starting March 28, 2026. The decision targets 'high-risk platforms' including TikTok, Facebook, Instagram, X, YouTube, and Roblox. The government aims to protect young users from online risks such as pornography, cyberbullying, online fraud, and addiction. Indonesia’s Communications and Digital Minister, Meutya Hafid, stated that accounts of under-16s will be gradually deactivated until social media companies meet certain regulatory obligations. The move follows a growing global trend, after countries like Australia, Spain, France, and Italy have introduced similar measures. Social media companies, including Meta and YouTube, expressed support for safer online experiences but emphasized the importance of parental control and avoiding driving teens to less regulated platforms. According to a 2024 survey, nearly 80% of Indonesia’s population uses the internet, including

item.title: BBC apologizes for airing racial slur during BAFTAs ceremony
item.description: The BBC has expressed profound regret over a serious broadcasting error during the BAFTAs on February 22, where a racial slur was aired live as Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo presented an award. The offensive language was uttered involuntarily by John Davidson, a Tourette's campaigner and the inspiration for the film 'I Swear'. Despite being symptomatic of his condition, the slur was broadcast to viewers for over 12 hours before being removed from BBC iPlayer. Outgoing BBC Director General Tim Davie acknowledged the mistake in a letter to Dame Caroline Dinenage, emphasizing that no one in the on-site broadcast truck was aware of the slur at the time, and therefore no editorial decision allowed it to air intentionally. Both Jordan and Lindo continued their presentation after pausing briefly. Davidson expressed that he was 'deeply mortified' by the incident. The controversy overshadowed the film's recognition, with Robert Aramayo winning the best actor award. BBC Chief Content Office

item.title: France Plans to Expand Nuclear Arsenal and Strengthen European Defense Cooperation
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced that France will increase the number of its nuclear warheads and enable temporary deployment of nuclear-armed aircraft to allied countries for military exercises. The decision is part of a broader strategy to 'Europeanize' France's nuclear deterrence capabilities in response to evolving global security threats. Macron delivered this statement during a major speech at the Île Longue nuclear submarine base, emphasizing the involvement of several European countries, including Belgium, Denmark, Germany, Greece, Sweden, the Netherlands, Poland, and the United Kingdom. London and Berlin are expected to play especially important roles in coordinating the program. European Union leaders have already expressed interest in collaborating with France on the details of this initiative. Macron stressed that the move is necessary to strengthen Europe’s defense posture amid the disintegration of established rules governing the use of nuclear weapons and risin

item.title: Stephen Hibbert, Known for 'Pulp Fiction' Role, Passes Away at 68
item.description: Stephen Hibbert, the actor recognized for portraying 'The Gimp' in Quentin Tarantino's cult classic 'Pulp Fiction,' passed away at 68 in Denver, Colorado, on March 2, 2026, following a heart attack. His family is awaiting an autopsy to determine further details. Beyond his iconic role, Hibbert had a diverse career in entertainment, contributing as a writer for 'Late Night with David Letterman,' working on 259 episodes from 1984 to 1986. He also wrote for television series including 'Boy Meets World' and 'Mad TV.' In addition to 'Pulp Fiction,' Hibbert appeared in films such as 'Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me,' 'The Cat in the Hat,' and 'National Treasure: Book of Secrets.' In a 2024 interview, he reflected on the sudden fan recognition after 'Pulp Fiction's' release, describing the overwhelming attention he received. Hibbert was married to actress Julia Sweeney from 1989 to 1994 and remained connected with the film community. He is survived by his three children: Ronnie, Rosalin

item.title: Beyond Nuclear Condemns US and Israeli Military Strikes on Iran Amid Nuclear Negotiations
item.description:
Beyond Nuclear, a nuclear advocacy organization, issued a strong condemnation of the recent military attacks on Iran carried out by the United States and Israel, two nuclear-armed nations. The statement, released on February 28, 2026, argues that these strikes occurred at a critical moment when diplomatic negotiations were advancing toward a new nuclear verification agreement with Iran. International specialist Linda Pentz Gunter described the attacks as illegal, dangerous, and provocative, warning that they could escalate into a broader regional conflict, potentially involving nuclear weapons, with devastating global consequences.
The article notes that Iran, an NPT signatory, has consistently denied pursuing nuclear weapons and is permitted to develop civilian nuclear programs. Prior to earlier US-Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities in June (likely 2025), the IAEA had found no evidence of weapons development. Iran's current uranium enrichment is reported at 60% or possibly

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Threatens Global Energy and Supply Chains
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route for the Persian Gulf, has emerged as a major threat to the global economy. Although stock markets like the S&P 500 and FTSE 100 have shown minimal movement, energy and supply chain experts warn that the economic impact will likely intensify over time. The shutdown is disrupting natural gas exports from Qatar, a key supplier, potentially triggering inflation in Europe and Asia as countries scramble to secure energy. The situation extends beyond gas; Qatar also produces a third of the world's helium, essential for MRI machines and certain quantum computers. The Gulf region is similarly affected, as limited pipeline capacity means that oil from Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Iraq, and Abu Dhabi cannot be shipped efficiently, risking field shutdowns or spills. Additionally, the dependence of Gulf economies on foreign workers raises social concerns if migration patterns change due to safety threats. Overall, the longer the Strait of Horm

item.title: AI Liquid Biopsy Detects Early Liver Fibrosis and Cirrhosis Before Symptoms
item.description: Researchers at Johns Hopkins Kimmel Cancer Center have developed an AI-driven blood test capable of detecting early liver fibrosis and cirrhosis years before symptoms appear. The technology, called fragmentome analysis, examines genome-wide patterns of cell-free DNA (cfDNA) fragments circulating in the blood. Unlike traditional liquid biopsies that look for specific mutations, this method analyzes how DNA fragments are broken and distributed, capturing hidden signals of overall health. Using whole-genome sequencing of cfDNA from over 1,500 individuals with liver conditions and other medical issues, the AI system identifies fragmentation patterns linked to disease. This approach showed high sensitivity for early liver disease detection, offering the potential to intervene before fibrosis progresses to cirrhosis or increases the risk of liver cancer. The researchers also created a fragmentome-based comorbidity index, which predicted overall survival and could be applied to other chronic

item.title: Ultrafast Electron Transfer Driven by Molecular Vibrations in Solar Materials
item.description: Researchers at the University of Cambridge have observed electrons moving across solar materials in just 18 femtoseconds, revealing a process much faster than traditional theories predicted. The study shows that tiny vibrations in molecules act as a 'molecular catapult,' propelling electrons in a coherent, directional burst rather than allowing slow, random diffusion. The experiments involved a polymer donor placed next to a non-fullerene acceptor under conditions that were expected to impede charge transfer, yet electrons crossed the interface almost instantaneously. These vibrations mix electronic states, effectively pushing the electron across the molecular boundary and initiating a new vibration in the acceptor molecule. This ultrafast charge separation challenges long-standing assumptions in solar material design, which previously emphasized large energy differences and strong coupling for rapid electron transfer. The findings open up new design principles for more efficient light

item.title: Study Reveals a Massive Flat Structure of Matter Surrounding the Milky Way
item.description: Astronomers have long been puzzled by the fact that most galaxies near the Milky Way appear to be moving away rather than being pulled inward by its gravity. A new study from the University of Groningen, led by PhD graduate Ewoud Wempe, provides a compelling explanation. Using advanced computer simulations based on the early universe's conditions and measurements of the cosmic microwave background, researchers discovered that our galaxy sits within a gigantic, flat sheet of matter spanning tens of millions of light-years. This structure contains both ordinary matter and dark matter, while enormous cosmic voids lie above and below it. The simulations accurately reproduce the observed positions and velocities of galaxies near the Local Group, including the Milky Way and Andromeda. The flat distribution of mass within this sheet counterbalances the gravitational pull of the Local Group, allowing surrounding galaxies to drift outward. This virtual model, described as a 'twin' of our local

item.title: Four House Democrats Vote Against War Powers Resolution Amid Lobby Influence
item.description: On March 6, 2026, four Democratic members of the U.S. House of Representatives voted against a bipartisan War Powers Resolution proposed by Thomas Massie. The resolution sought to require congressional approval before any further military escalations, aiming to increase legislative oversight over decisions of war and conflict. These four Democrats, who have received substantial financial support from the pro-Israel lobby AIPAC, opposed the measure. According to reports, the lobbying group spent over $9.3 million supporting these representatives. The vote has sparked discussions about the influence of lobbying and campaign contributions on congressional decisions, highlighting concerns about the intersection of foreign policy, money in politics, and democratic accountability. Critics argue that the resolution’s failure reflects how lobbying can shape policy outcomes, while supporters of the legislators maintain that their positions reflect strategic or foreign policy considerations. Thi

item.title: Defense Contractors Commit to Boost Weapons Output After Iran Strikes
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that major U.S. weapons manufacturers have agreed to ramp up production to replenish munitions stockpiles depleted by recent military strikes on Iran. The White House hosted executives from leading defense firms, including Lockheed Martin and RTX (Raytheon’s parent company), to discuss accelerating output amid ongoing conflicts in the Middle East. These strikes involved advanced weaponry such as Tomahawk cruise missiles, F-35 stealth fighters, and low-cost one-way attack drones, significantly drawing down U.S. reserves. Trump emphasized on social media that America possesses a 'virtually unlimited supply' of munitions, enabling sustained military operations indefinitely. Meanwhile, the Pentagon, under Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg, is preparing a supplemental budget request of approximately $50 billion to fund replacement of expended weapons and support further operations. Sources indicate the meeting focused on urging contractors to prioritiz

item.title: Celebration of Life Honors Rev. Jesse Jackson's Legacy in Chicago
item.description: The celebration of life for civil rights leader Rev. Jesse Jackson is taking place in Chicago, marking a final public tribute before a private service. Jackson, who passed away in February at 84, is remembered for reshaping American politics, advancing civil rights, and inspiring generations with his moral vision. Prominent speakers included former Presidents Barack Obama and Joe Biden, who praised Jackson's dedication to justice, hope, and political transformation. Obama emphasized the importance of continuing Jackson’s work to uphold democratic principles, while Biden reflected on Jackson's enduring influence on hope and civic engagement. Other speakers included NBA legend Isiah Thomas, who highlighted Jackson's personal impact, and James Reynolds Jr., who credited Jackson with transforming opportunities for Black professionals on Wall Street. Musical tributes, including Jennifer Hudson’s powerful rendition of 'A Change Is Gonna Come,' added a soulful and emotional layer to the cerem

item.title: Environmental and Resource Challenges in Venezuela's Orinoco River Basin
item.description: Venezuela’s Orinoco River Basin is one of the most biodiverse regions in the world, home to river dolphins, endangered Orinoco crocodiles, and over a thousand species of freshwater fish and birds. However, this ecological treasure is under severe threat from both legal and illegal resource extraction. Over the past decade, illegal mining accelerated under former President Nicolás Maduro, especially in the Orinoco Mining Arc, an area covering about 12% of the country including national parks and Indigenous territories. Criminal gangs and armed groups control many mining operations, leading to deforestation, soil erosion, mercury contamination, and the spread of tropical diseases. These operations also disrupt local communities and contribute to serious human rights abuses, including child labor and sexual assault. Venezuela also holds some of the world’s largest proven crude oil reserves in the same region. With the removal of Maduro in 2026, U.S. authorities indicated intentions to con

item.title: Global Implications of the Iran Conflict: Strategic Alignments and Risks
item.description: The article analyzes the international dimensions of the ongoing conflict involving Iran, highlighting its potential to disrupt global energy supplies and escalate tensions in Europe. It examines the strategic roles of major powers: China, Russia, and Iran form a bloc challenging Western dominance, with China primarily focused on regional strategic positioning rather than direct military involvement in the Middle East. Russia is portrayed as alarmed by the war, navigating internal political dynamics and balancing negotiations with the U.S. and military strategies in Ukraine. The piece critiques the U.S. administration, describing chaotic decision-making, limited industrial capacity for sustained military campaigns, and contradictory messaging. It also underscores the strategic importance of missile capabilities, the potential consequences of failed military actions for U.S. leadership, and lessons from historical conflicts against nations lacking nuclear deterrents. Overall, the articl

item.title: China’s cautious stance on Iran reflects strategic limits and transactional diplomacy
item.description: China has maintained a restrained position regarding the U.S.-Israeli military campaign in Iran, balancing doctrinal principles against its limited influence in the region. While China officially opposes regime change and external interventions, its response combined diplomatic protest with precautionary measures, including urging evacuations and cautioning citizens in Israel. Unlike its strong support for Pakistan during the 2025 India-Pakistan conflict, China has limited obligations toward Iran, providing selective military and dual-use technology but no formal defense guarantees. Observing the conflict allows China to monitor U.S. military capabilities, which informs its broader strategic planning in the Indo-Pacific. China’s economic and strategic interests are more aligned with Gulf nations than with Iran, making Iran’s destabilization inconvenient but not critical. The ongoing U.S.-Israeli strikes, coupled with regional tensions involving Russia, Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Latin

item.title: Trump Replaces Interior Secretary Kristi Noem Amid Controversy Over Immigration Policy and Misconduct
item.description: On March 6, 2026, former South Dakota Governor and U.S. Secretary of the Interior Kristi Noem was dismissed by President Trump due to widespread criticism over her role in harsh immigration enforcement policies and alleged personal misconduct. Lawmakers from both parties condemned her luxurious lifestyle, extramarital relationship with her deputy Corey Lewandowski, and misuse of public funds, including the purchase of private jets and lavish accommodations. Noem delegated significant authority to immigration officer Gregory Bovino, who oversaw operations that resulted in the deaths of two Americans, Nicole Renee Good and Alex Pretti, undermining her public narrative of combating 'domestic terrorists.' A controversial $220 million advertising campaign urging immigrants to leave the U.S. further damaged her credibility. Investigations revealed violent actions by immigration officers, including Charles Exum, whose shootings were initially defended by Noem, falsely labeling victims as 'nat

item.title: Ramaphosa Criticizes Trump’s White Refugee Policy and Misunderstanding of South Africa
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa strongly criticized former U.S. President Donald Trump’s refugee policy that favored white Afrikaners, calling it 'racist' and highlighting Trump’s lack of understanding about South Africa’s history and current reality. Ramaphosa described a meeting with Trump as a 'spectacle' and an 'ambush,' where Trump accused him of failing to stop 'white genocide' in the country. Trump reportedly showed misinterpreted media and printed articles to support these claims. Despite welcoming 59 white South African refugees and imposing tariffs while cutting aid, Ramaphosa refuted allegations of racial persecution or land grabs, emphasizing that white farmers are not being driven out or mistreated. Ramaphosa criticized the policy as a reflection of a biased and uninformed perspective, noting that South Africa poses no threat to the United States. The White House defended Trump’s actions, citing humanitarian intentions and highlighting the plight of Afrikaners, whi

item.title: Medix 5.5 Retinol Body Lotion Gains Praise from Amazon Reviewers for Improving Crepey and Sun-Damaged Skin
item.description: HuffPost highlights the Medix 5.5 Retinol Body Lotion Firming Moisturizer as a popular and affordable option for addressing crepey, sun-damaged, or sagging skin on the body. Priced at around $20 on Amazon, the lotion features retinol to help smooth wrinkles and improve texture, combined with ferulic acid to combat sun damage and antioxidants. Additional ingredients like licorice root, vitamin E, shea butter, and botanicals provide hydration, soothing, and nourishment, making it suitable for dry winter skin or sensitive types. It's available in scented and fragrance-free versions and works for all skin types. Numerous Amazon reviewers, including those in their 60s, 70s, and 80s, report noticeable improvements in skin elasticity, reduced crepiness on arms, hands, legs, and elbows, faded dark spots, and overall smoother, healthier-looking skin after consistent use, often within days to weeks. Some compare it favorably to more expensive alternatives and note it absorbs well without irritat

item.title: Iran Reports Execution of Revolutionary Guard General Amid Allegations of Espionage
item.description: Iran has reportedly executed General Ismail Qaani, commander of the Quds Force within the Revolutionary Guard, over alleged long-term collaboration with Israel's Mossad. Qaani, aged 67, is said to have provided intelligence on the whereabouts of Iran's supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, before an Israeli attack. Sources claim he survived multiple targeted attacks against Iranian and allied leaders, raising suspicions of espionage. Reports suggest Chinese military intelligence intercepted communications linking Qaani to U.S. and Israeli intelligence. Over the years, numerous high-ranking figures in the so-called Resistance Axis, including Hezbollah and Hamas leaders, were killed in operations that Qaani allegedly narrowly escaped. Qaani assumed leadership of the Quds Force in 2020 following the U.S. killing of Qassem Suleimani. While some reports have not independently verified his execution, the general’s repeated survival in life-threatening incidents has fueled speculation of insider betr

item.title: Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks arrested for suspected DUI in Scottsdale
item.description: Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks was arrested early Friday morning in Scottsdale, Arizona, on suspicion of driving under the influence. Police conducted a routine traffic stop at 1:33 a.m., leading to Brooks’ arrest. He was transported to the Scottsdale City Jail and released around 3:30 a.m. following the booking process. The Suns organization acknowledged the situation, stating they are gathering more information and had no further comment at this time. Brooks, who had been enjoying one of the best seasons of his career, had recently suffered a broken left hand. He was averaging a career-high 20.9 points per game with a 44.0% field goal percentage. Originally drafted by the Memphis Grizzlies in 2017, Brooks later played for the Houston Rockets before being traded to the Suns in a deal involving Kevin Durant. Brooks has been involved in on-court incidents with LeBron James, including ejections and physical skirmishes during games in December. Despite recent setbacks, he remains a ke

item.title: Hayden AI Sues Former CEO Over Alleged Data Theft and Resume Fraud
item.description: Hayden AI, a San Francisco-based startup specializing in spatial analytics for urban planning, has filed a lawsuit against its former co-founder and CEO, Chris Carson. The company alleges that in the days leading up to his removal in September 2024, Carson engaged in a series of fraudulent activities. The lawsuit claims he secretly copied 41GB of company emails, forged board signatures, sold more than $1.2 million in company stock without authorization, and misallocated personal expenses. Additionally, Hayden AI accuses Carson of falsifying key parts of his resume, including claims of a PhD from Waseda University and military service, which the company asserts are entirely fabricated. The startup, currently valued at an estimated $464 million, is seeking preliminary injunctive relief to ensure that the allegedly stolen data is returned or destroyed. Commenters have noted that the situation reflects broader issues in the AI industry, including insufficient due diligence in hiring and th

item.title: Top Adviser to NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani Advises British Progressive Politicians on Campaign Strategies
item.description: Morris Katz, a 26-year-old key strategist behind New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's successful 2025 mayoral campaign, traveled to the United Kingdom in February 2026 to meet with progressive politicians from the Labour and Green parties. Katz, who has not joined Mamdani's administration but remains a close adviser, met with figures including Labour MPs Rosie Wrighting and Gordon McKee, as well as Rowenna Davis, a Labour candidate for mayor of Croydon. The meetings, initiated by UK operatives, focused on campaign strategies, messaging, and social media tactics that helped Mamdani win by emphasizing affordability issues like free public transit and childcare, funded by taxes on the wealthy. Katz described his efforts as part of an international fight against oligarchic and far-right interests. He has continued virtual consultations and plans further engagement with Green Party leadership, though his UK work was unpaid and he is prioritizing U.S. midterm elections. This reflects growing

item.title: National Democrats Hesitate on Major Spending for Texas Senate Race
item.description: National Democrats are grappling with whether to invest heavily in Texas to support James Talarico's U.S. Senate campaign amid recent primary successes. Talarico, a state representative who won the Democratic nomination, has sparked optimism after strong primary results, but party strategists question the high cost of competing in the historically Republican state. A Democratic consultant noted that $150 million for Texas might be better allocated to cheaper battlegrounds like Iowa, Montana, or Nebraska to secure House and Senate majorities. Senate Majority PAC emphasized that while paths to victory run through states like Ohio and North Carolina, Texas remains in play. Talarico's campaign has raised significant funds from grassroots donors, positioning him as a populist fighter against billionaire influence, though Republicans mock his chances and highlight his progressive stances on issues like borders and gender. Some GOP insiders privately acknowledge the risk, citing recent spendi

item.title: Research Highlights Underdiagnosis of Sleep Apnea in Women and the Need for Targeted Detection
item.description: Sleep apnea, once thought to primarily affect older, heavier men, is increasingly recognized as a significant and often undetected condition in women, especially during perimenopause and menopause. Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) occurs when the upper airway collapses during sleep, causing repeated oxygen drops and brief awakenings. Hormonal changes, particularly the decline in estrogen and progesterone after menopause, increase women’s susceptibility to OSA by reducing airway protection and altering fat distribution. Women may experience symptoms differently than men, often presenting with fatigue, insomnia, mood changes, headaches, or restless sleep, rather than classic loud snoring or excessive daytime sleepiness. These subtle presentations frequently lead to misdiagnosis or delayed diagnosis, as existing screening tools were largely developed based on male or mixed populations. Even mild apnea, with roughly 15 breathing disruptions per hour, can have serious health consequences, incl

item.title: Nvidia's China Permit Issues Fail to Undermine Strong Bull Case
item.description: Recent reports indicate that Chinese customs officials have instructed agents not to permit entry of Nvidia's advanced H200 AI chips, sparking concerns among investors about the company's exposure to the Chinese market. Despite U.S. export approval being granted this week under the Trump administration, Beijing's stringent directives suggest a de facto ban, at least temporarily, as authorities met with local tech firms to discourage purchases unless absolutely necessary. The motives behind this move remain unclear, potentially serving as a bargaining tactic amid escalating U.S.-China tech tensions or an effort to bolster domestic chipmakers. Nvidia, a dominant force in AI semiconductors, derives a notable portion of revenue from China, making such restrictions a potential headwind. However, the Seeking Alpha article argues that these permit fears do not fundamentally alter Nvidia's bullish investment case. The author emphasizes Nvidia's unparalleled leadership in AI GPU technology, wit

item.title: Jaylen Brown Rejects Beverly Hills Apology Amid Renewed Claims of Racial Profiling in Police Practices
item.description: Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown rejected an apology from the city of Beverly Hills after police shut down his private invite-only brand event for 741 Performance during NBA All-Star Weekend on February 14, 2026. The event, held at a mansion in Trousdale Estates owned by Oakley founder James Jannard, was halted around 7 p.m. following a resident complaint about excessive vehicles and parking violations. Police cited code enforcement issues, including lack of a required public assembly permit for over 50 attendees, a generator, metal detector, and other setups. Brown, in a viral video, expressed feeling targeted and confused, questioning why his calm panel on culture, leadership, and the future was disrupted. The city initially claimed a permit was needed and denied, but later apologized for inaccurate information, admitting no application existed and no prior violations were on record. Brown expressed offense, cited financial losses and reputational harm to his brand, and indicated he

item.title: Selena Gomez playfully kisses Benny Blanco’s foot during podcast appearance
item.description: During a recent episode of Benny Blanco’s podcast 'Friends Keep Secrets,' Selena Gomez surprised listeners with a playful gesture by kissing her husband’s foot in the middle of a conversation. The 33-year-old singer was seated on the floor next to Blanco, who was relaxing on the couch, when she leaned over and kissed his index toe. Blanco paused briefly before smiling and joking with co-hosts Lil Dicky (Dave Burd) and Kristin Batalucco. The moment was lighthearted, with Gomez playfully slapping Blanco’s leg and continuing the affectionate exchange by resting her cheek against his toes. The podcast episode also touched on the couple’s relationship, with Gomez praising their bond as 'healthy' and 'one of the greatest things in life,' while Blanco explained he usually keeps public displays of affection minimal to allow Gomez to maintain professionalism during interviews. The playful foot-kissing moment followed earlier online attention on Blanco’s feet after viewers noticed them looking d

item.title: Christina Applegate recalls leaving Brad Pitt for Sebastian Bach at the 1989 MTV Awards
item.description: In her memoir 'You With the Sad Eyes,' Christina Applegate recounts a pivotal moment from the 1989 MTV Video Music Awards. At 17, during the height of her fame from 'Married… with Children,' she attended the Los Angeles ceremony with Brad Pitt as her date. Applegate describes feeling confident and glamorous in a Ceil Chapman gown, which gave her the courage to leave Pitt at the event and walk out with Skid Row frontman Sebastian Bach instead. She reflects on the dynamic at the time, noting that Pitt had not yet achieved major stardom and was still establishing his acting career, which would later explode with 'Thelma & Louise' in 1991. According to Applegate, Pitt was understandably upset and had to drive her mother and a friend home, leading to years of minimal contact between them. Eventually, the two reconciled, with Pitt forgiving her youthful decision. Applegate had previously hinted at this story during a 2015 interview on 'Watch What Happens Live,' without revealing Bach’s ident

item.title: U.S. Military Presence in Gulf States Increases Regional Security Risks
item.description: Before recent U.S.-Iran tensions escalated, Gulf countries believed hosting American military bases would strengthen their security by deterring attacks. However, recent events have shown that these bases primarily serve U.S. strategic interests rather than the protection of host nations. The presence of American infrastructure has inadvertently exposed Gulf states to potential Iranian retaliation, undermining their security. While no Gulf countries have yet retaliated against Iran, some have considered it, with Saudi Arabia positioned as the regional leader capable of coordinating potential military action with the United States. Regional rivalries, such as that between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, could influence whether coordinated action occurs. Gulf states share a common concern about perceived Iranian aggression, which is also framed in historical and sectarian terms following the 1979 Iranian Revolution and subsequent geopolitical developments like the Abraham Acco

item.title: Asian Stock Markets Plunge Amid Rising Middle East Tensions
item.description: Asian stock markets have experienced significant drops as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalate. The conflict involving Iran has triggered sharp declines across major Asian indices, with Seoul's Kospi index falling nearly 10%, marking its largest drop in 15 years since the conflict began. Measures to temporarily halt trading could not prevent losses. In Tokyo, the Nikkei fell 4%, while Hong Kong and Singapore saw decreases of 3.1% and 2.8% respectively. Key corporations, heavily reliant on Gulf oil and natural gas, also suffered; Hyundai dropped almost 14%, LG Energy Solutions fell 10%, and Samsung declined 9.2%. Japanese firms including Toyota, SoftBank, and Advantest also faced notable losses. The instability reflects the economic vulnerabilities of countries dependent on Middle Eastern oil, particularly as the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical shipping route. Investors reacted to Iran's unexpected military responses and the broadening of regional operations to neighbor

item.title: Trump Supporters in Pennsylvania Show Mixed Reactions to Military Action Against Iran
item.description: Residents in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania, a Republican stronghold, have shown divided opinions following U.S. military strikes against Iran under President Donald Trump. Some voters express concern that the actions contradict Trump's campaign promises to avoid foreign conflicts, emphasizing that their support was based on an 'America First' anti-war stance. Critics argue that the strikes could put American lives at risk and clash with the administration’s principle of 'peace through strength.' Conversely, other supporters view the military action as necessary, framing it as a protective measure that aligns with confronting dangerous regimes and prioritizing U.S. interests. The issue also resonates beyond traditional Republican voters, including members of the Iranian diaspora and centrists who support strong action against Iran's leadership. National polling indicates broader skepticism about the strikes, with 43% of Americans opposing the moves, suggesting potential political challenges

item.title: Billy Idol Discusses Overcoming Heroin Addiction and Life Lessons
item.description: Rock icon Billy Idol recently opened up about his struggles with drug addiction, explaining that during his battle with heroin, he briefly turned to crack as a way to stop using heroin. Idol shared these revelations during a podcast appearance on Club Random with Bill Maher, emphasizing that while it was a dangerous choice, it ultimately helped him break free from heroin. In his new documentary, 'Billy Idol Should Be Dead,' the singer reflects on his tumultuous career, near-death experiences, and reckless lifestyle, including a severe motorcycle accident in 1990 and a near-fatal heroin overdose in 1984. Idol recounted how drug use was deeply ingrained in the 1980s rock scene, beginning with his first experience with acid at just 12 years old. Over time, fatherhood and self-reflection led him to adopt a more disciplined lifestyle. Today, he describes himself as 'California sober,' occasionally using cannabis-based products but avoiding cocaine for more than 20 years. The documentary pre

item.title: Military Forces Increasingly Exploit Civilian Security Cameras for Reconnaissance
item.description: Recent research by the security firm Check Point reveals that civilian security cameras are increasingly being targeted as tools for military reconnaissance in conflicts from Ukraine to Iran. Iranian state-linked hackers reportedly attempted to hijack hundreds of consumer-grade cameras, coinciding with missile and drone strikes in Israel, Qatar, and Cyprus. Similar tactics have been used by Israel, Russia, and Ukraine, demonstrating a growing reliance on inexpensive, networked civilian devices to gather real-time intelligence. The attacks exploited known vulnerabilities in Hikvision and Dahua cameras, many of which were years old but remained unpatched due to users neglecting updates. These hacked cameras provide militaries with detailed ground-level surveillance that can be used to plan strikes, track enemy movements, and assess damage after attacks. Experts note that this method is cheaper, stealthier, and sometimes more informative than traditional reconnaissance tools such as satel

item.title: Tate McRae and Jack Hughes Are in an Exclusive Relationship
item.description: Pop singer Tate McRae, 22, and Team USA hockey player Jack Hughes, 24, have reportedly started dating exclusively, according to a source cited by Us Weekly. The couple was first spotted together in New York City in December, and they have been spending time together since late last year. The source confirmed that the relationship is still in its early stages but is progressing positively, with McRae describing it as going well so far. Hughes reportedly initiated contact via Instagram, and McRae finds him both 'really cool' and 'really sweet.' They have enjoyed shared activities, and McRae has been attending Hughes' hockey games to support him. Hughes recently gained international attention for scoring the gold medal-winning goal for Team USA in men’s ice hockey at the Winter Olympics, marking the country’s first gold in the sport since 1980. Following the match, images of Hughes celebrating with a bloodied smile and a broken front tooth circulated widely, though he has joked that the i

item.title: Cardi B and Megan Thee Stallion Reunite for Surprise Performance in Houston
item.description: During her Little Miss Drama Tour stop in Houston on March 4, 2026, Cardi B delighted fans by bringing Megan Thee Stallion on stage for a surprise performance. The concert, held at the Toyota Center, featured songs from both Cardi B’s debut album 'Invasion of Privacy' and her latest record 'Am I the Drama,' which inspired the tour’s title. The highlight of the night came when Megan Thee Stallion joined Cardi for their chart-topping hit 'WAP,' sending the crowd into an uproar. The collaboration had particular resonance in Houston, as it is Megan’s hometown, making the moment a personal triumph for both artists and their fans. In addition to 'WAP,' the pair also delivered a live performance of their recent single 'Bongos,' further energizing the atmosphere. The appearance added to the growing list of joint performances between the two rap stars, following a similar surprise in 2024 when Cardi appeared on stage with Megan during her Hot Girl Summer Tour in New York. Their repeated collabo

item.title: Heatherwick Studio Proposes Curved Residential Towers for Seoul’s Daegyo Apartments
item.description: Heatherwick Studio has unveiled an innovative plan to redevelop the Daegyo Apartments in Yeouido, Seoul. The project aims to replace the aging 1975-era complex with four new residential towers featuring soft, rolling rooflines inspired by the nearby mountains. Departing from the conventional straight high-rise design, the proposed towers emphasize curves and texture to create a warmer, more inviting residential environment. The redevelopment would expand the site from 576 homes to approximately 900, with significant input from hundreds of resident union members. At street level, the design focuses on calm, pedestrian-friendly spaces, incorporating raised site edges to buffer traffic and sunken courtyards to offer tranquil urban retreats. The ground floor is intended to be accessible to both residents and the public, integrating community facilities such as play areas and welfare services. By blending architectural innovation with community-oriented planning, the project aims to provide

item.title: Analysis of U.S. Patriot Missile Inventory and Potential Shortages
item.description: The United States currently relies on the PAC-3 MSE (Patriot Advanced Capability-3 Missile with Enhanced Segment) as its primary interceptor for high-priority threats. Production of this missile began in small quantities in 2014, scaling up to full production in 2018. From 2015 to 2025, a total of approximately 4,620 PAC-3 MSE missiles were produced, with 847 transferred to Ukraine. Assuming none were used elsewhere, this leaves around 3,773 in U.S. inventory. Military analysts estimate that in a high-intensity conflict scenario like the hypothetical '12-Day War,' with Iran launching 60 ballistic missiles per day and two interceptors fired per incoming missile, the U.S. would exhaust its stock in roughly 10 days. These calculations assume all missiles are deployed in Israel and nearby Gulf states, though in reality, some are stationed in other theaters such as INDOPACOM and EUCOM. The analysis highlights potential vulnerabilities in U.S. missile defense capabilities if faced with susta

item.title: The Decline of Western Influence in the Middle East and the Risks of Escalation
item.description: The article analyzes the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, emphasizing Iran's resilience against Western and Israeli interventions. The author argues that Iran will not collapse like Iraq, Syria, or Libya, due to its long-standing civilization and societal strength, even if its current regime falls. The piece critiques the U.S. strategy, particularly the assassination of Iran's leader, labeling it a tactical but strategically disastrous move. The author highlights the fragility of Western-backed local regimes and the limitations of imperial protection, noting that missile and drone attacks demonstrate the vulnerability of allied nations. Israel is portrayed as pursuing an aggressive regional strategy that is unsustainable in the long term due to its small population, lack of resources, and dependence on Western support. The article predicts that failures in Iran could accelerate the collapse of U.S. influence and the petrodollar system, while the potential use of nuclear weapons wou

item.title: Roman Water Systems and Public Hygiene: How Infrastructure Sustained the Empire
item.description: Long before modern plumbing, ancient Rome had developed an advanced water and sanitation system that set it apart from most of the world. Starting in 312 B.C. with the construction of the Aqua Appia, Romans brought clean water from distant springs into the city through underground aqueducts, eventually building a network of eleven major aqueducts delivering over a million cubic meters of water daily. This water supplied public fountains, private homes, baths, and gardens, creating a continuous flow system rather than mere storage. The distribution was carefully managed, with the curator aquarum overseeing allocation and addressing issues such as illegal diversions and theft by wealthy citizens. Public baths, numbering over nine hundred by the fourth century, were centers of hygiene, socializing, and leisure, highlighting the cultural importance of water. Equally vital was the Cloaca Maxima, a massive sewer system that drained rainwater, domestic waste, and bath water, making the city l

item.title: Emma Watson Seen Socializing with Entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Baillères
item.description: Emma Watson has been spotted multiple times with Mexican entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Baillères, sparking public interest in a potential romantic connection. Sightings of the pair began circulating in early 2026 after attending a wedding in Mexico together. More recently, they were photographed at an airport in March by the Mexican magazine Quién, as well as in locations such as Courchevel in the French Alps, Punta Mita on Mexico’s Pacific coast, and various sites in Mexico City. One image circulating online appears to show them displaying affection publicly, though neither Watson nor Hevia Baillères has confirmed any relationship. Gonzalo Hevia Baillères comes from one of Mexico’s wealthiest families, including ties to mining company Industrias Peñoles, Grupo BAL, and the luxury department store chain El Palacio de Hierro. Forbes ranks his family among the richest in Latin America, with estimated wealth between $6.65 billion and $19.7 billion. Hevia Baillères has pursued his own venture

item.title: US Justice Department Releases Previously Missing FBI Interview Summaries Containing Unverified Allegations Against Trump from Epstein Files
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has released additional FBI documents that were absent from the initial public batch of Jeffrey Epstein files, following reports by media outlets including NBC News. These files, which had been incorrectly marked as 'duplicative' in the department's spreadsheet, include summaries and notes from three 2019 FBI interviews (conducted on August 7, August 20, and October 16) with a woman from South Carolina. She alleged that Jeffrey Epstein sexually assaulted her on Hilton Head Island around 1984 when she was 13, and claimed further abuse by Epstein and several associates, including then-president Donald Trump. Only the summary of the initial July 24, 2019 interview was in the earlier release and made no mention of Trump. The woman’s name is redacted throughout the documents. The Justice Department reiterated warnings that the Epstein files contain unverified, false, or sensationalist claims—some submitted just before the 2020 election—and emphasized that thes

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Threatens Global Aluminum Supply
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is severely impacting the global aluminum market. The blockade threatens both the supply of aluminum to the world and the delivery of alumina to smelters in the Persian Gulf. Stock prices are falling while aluminum prices are rising, with the price per ton increasing from $3,140 to $3,300, a 5% jump, nearing levels last seen in 2022. Gulf countries are key producers, accounting for 8.3% of global primary aluminum production and 14.5% of global exports. Energy costs, particularly natural gas, directly affect aluminum production costs, and the region’s largest producer, the United Arab Emirates, is heavily dependent on the Strait of Hormuz for exports. Even minor disruptions can create supply chain challenges for industries from automotive manufacturing to beverage cans. European, U.S., and Asian markets are already paying higher prices for alternative supply. Qatar’s public energy company announced a halt in aluminum production due to domestic gas sho

item.title: Trump Announces Potential US Naval Escorts and Financial Guarantees to Resume Shipping Through the Strait of Hormuz Amid Iranian Threats
item.description: On Tuesday, March 3, 2026, President Donald Trump announced plans to form naval escorts 'as soon as possible' to help stranded vessels in the Persian Gulf transit the Strait of Hormuz, which has seen severely reduced traffic due to heightened risks from the ongoing conflict involving Iran. He also pledged to provide financial guarantees at reasonable prices through the US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to cover risks that private insurers are unwilling to take, aiming to restore commercial shipping flows. However, less than 24 hours earlier, US Navy officials stated there was no realistic possibility of deploying escort ships and questioned their effectiveness against potential Iranian threats. The article draws parallels to the failed 2023 US and EU operations in the Red Sea against Houthi forces during the Gaza conflict, suggesting similar efforts would likely fail against Iran. Normally, 100 to 120 vessels (including tankers, LNG carriers, chemical ships, contai

item.title: Iran's Mosaic Defense Strategy: Designed to Prolong Conflict and Counter Rapid Decapitation Strikes by the US and Israel
item.description: The article examines Iran's 'mosaic defense' (defensa en mosaico descentralizada), a decentralized military architecture developed since the early 2000s to thwart quick, decisive victories by technologically superior adversaries like the United States and Israel. It argues that modern wars are won by the side that retains organized force after the initial blow, not the one that strikes first. Iran's strategy, formalized under IRGC General Mohammad Ali Jafari around 2005 and restructured into 32 semi-autonomous provincial commands, prevents 'decapitation' by dispersing authority so that no single strike can paralyze the system. Key components include the IRGC as the core, the Basij paramilitary for societal integration and local resistance, the conventional Artesh army for border defense, dispersed and mobile missile forces, layered air defenses (such as Bavar-373 and S-300 systems), and an external 'Axis of Resistance' network involving Hezbollah, Iraqi militias, and Yemen's Houthis co

item.title: Britney Spears Arrested in California on DUI Charges
item.description: Pop icon Britney Spears was arrested by the California Highway Patrol on Wednesday evening at approximately 9:30 pm on suspicion of driving under the influence. She was booked into custody at 3 am the following Thursday and later released. Spears is scheduled to appear in court on May 4, where the case will proceed. Official documents list her occupation as 'celebrity,' and while authorities have not released additional details about the incident, the arrest comes amid growing public concern over Spears' recent behavior and social media activity. In recent months, the singer's posts have sparked speculation among fans, and she recently disabled her Instagram account, a primary platform she used to communicate with the public. This disappearance added to the concerns over her well-being. Spears, who gained global fame in the late 1990s and early 2000s, has long had her personal life scrutinized alongside her music career. Her court appearance will provide clarity on the legal outcomes r

item.title: Middle East Conflict Raises Concerns Over Global Economic Stability
item.description: The ongoing military actions by the United States and Israel against Iran have caused a sharp rise in energy prices, prompting serious concerns about global economic stability. The British Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves, had intended to use her spring economic statement to convey confidence and stability, but the sudden escalation in the Middle East has disrupted these plans. The Office for Budget Responsibility has acknowledged that the conflict will have a severe impact on both the UK and global economies. Britain's economy is particularly vulnerable to rising oil and gas prices, which directly affect electricity costs. As energy prices remain high, inflation is unlikely to drop to the government's target of 2%, limiting the Bank of England's ability to lower interest rates. Additionally, government borrowing costs are rising due to higher yields on 10-year government bonds, reflecting increased military spending and budget deficits. Before the conflict, growth projection

item.title: Lily Allen References David Harbour and Breakup Themes During West End Girl Tour Performance
item.description: During her West End Girl tour stop at Glasgow Royal Concert Hall on March 2, 2026, British singer Lily Allen made a striking visual statement tied to the themes of her 2024 album. On stage, she wore a dress constructed from long pieces of green fabric printed to resemble purchase receipts allegedly linked to her former husband, actor David Harbour. The moment corresponded with the lyrics from her song “4chanStan,” which describes discovering a luxury handbag receipt possibly connected to infidelity. As part of the performance, Allen pulled the printed fabric from beside a stage bed and wrapped it around herself while singing the verse, visually portraying the emotional narrative of the song. Later, she interacted with stage props including a refrigerator, from which she retrieved another fabric printed with handwritten lyrics, using it as a head wrap to dramatize the chorus referencing 'a sad, sad man.' The performance illustrates the theatrical and autobiographical elements Allen has

item.title: Critique of Western Support for US-Israel Strikes on Iran in 2026
item.description: In this opinion article dated March 3, 2026, Dr. Binoy Kampmark argues that US allies have abandoned international law principles by endorsing or tolerating the joint US-Israeli military strikes on Iran that began on February 28, 2026. These strikes, which killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and targeted nuclear and missile facilities, are portrayed as an illegal preemptive attack violating UN Charter Article 2(4), which prohibits the threat or use of force against another state's territorial integrity. The author contrasts this with strong Western condemnation of Russia's actions in Ukraine and highlights hypocritical positions from countries like the UK, Australia, and Canada. For instance, Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong supported preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons without addressing the strikes' legality, while the UK framed its involvement as 'defensive' support for regional allies. Kampmark criticizes leaders such as Canada's Mark Carney for inc

item.title: India Considers Russian Oil to Reduce Dependence on Gulf Supplies Amid Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: India relies heavily on imported oil, with over half coming from the Middle East and passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The recent closure of this strategic waterway has created a potential crisis for the country, which imports 88% of its oil and has reserves that could last only about 50 days. Unlike China or Japan, India’s limited crude reserves make it highly vulnerable to supply disruptions. Indian refineries have expressed interest in restarting imports of Russian oil, which had been reduced due to U.S. pressure and punitive tariffs imposed last year. These tariffs were partially lifted following a trade agreement, but the Supreme Court later annulled the tariffs and the deal, creating uncertainty. In the meantime, refineries have also sought alternative routes, such as receiving oil from Saudi Arabia via the Red Sea, though this route is also threatened by regional instability. The Indian government now faces a critical decision: increase Russian oil imports to secure fuel sup

item.title: Former Green Bay Packers President and CEO Bob Harlan Passes Away at 89
item.description: Bob Harlan, who led the Green Bay Packers as president and CEO from 1989 to 2008, has died at the age of 89 following a brief illness. The team announced his passing on March 5, 2026, after his family informed them. Harlan had recently been hospitalized with pneumonia. During his tenure, he played a key role in revitalizing the franchise, which had struggled prior to his leadership. He hired general manager Ron Wolf, who orchestrated the trade for quarterback Brett Favre from the Atlanta Falcons, leading to consistent winning seasons from 1992 to 2004 and a Super Bowl victory in 1997. Harlan also oversaw a major $295 million renovation of Lambeau Field, preserving and modernizing the iconic stadium while ensuring the publicly owned team's long-term stability. NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell praised him for restoring the Packers' tradition of excellence and contributing to the league's success in small-market cities. Brett Favre credited Harlan's support during challenges in his career a

item.title: European Airlines See Short-Term Gains Amid Middle East Airspace Closures
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has caused significant disruptions in the region's major international air hubs, such as Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha. This has unintentionally benefited European airlines, as travelers are forced to seek alternative routes between Europe and Asia. Airlines including Air France, Lufthansa, KLM, and British Airways have responded by adding additional flights and increasing seating capacity. For example, Air France expanded its flights to Bangkok, Delhi, and Singapore, raising its March schedule to 908 flights to Asia compared to 800 in February. Lufthansa and KLM have also added dozens of flights, while British Airways has scheduled nearly 100 extra routes. Despite these gains, rising oil prices remain a concern. Jet fuel costs have surged by 25% within just four days, affecting overall operational expenses. European airlines have mitigated part of this impact through fuel surcharges, which add roughly $32 per passenger on long-haul flights like New

item.title: California’s Sierra Nevada Snowpack Declines Rapidly Due to Early Spring Heat Wave
item.description: In late February and early March 2026, California experienced a dramatic reduction in its Sierra Nevada snowpack following an intense winter heat wave. NASA satellite imagery reveals that snow levels that had accumulated after major storms in mid-February diminished substantially in less than ten days, with temperatures soaring 15 to 20 degrees above normal. According to UC Agriculture and Natural Resources climate scientist Daniel Swain, the speed of the snow loss is extraordinary. The northern Sierra currently holds only 38% of its average snowpack, while the southern Sierra is at 83%, a notable drop from earlier levels near 98%. The reduced snowpack has significant implications for California, which relies on it for roughly 30% of its water supply. Experts attribute the trend to climate change, as warmer winters increasingly cause precipitation to fall as rain rather than snow and shift snowlines higher in elevation. Continued above-average temperatures may further diminish snow res

item.title: Israeli Government Faces Lawsuits from Contractors and Influencers Over Unpaid Propaganda Work During Gaza Conflict
item.description: Israel's international propaganda apparatus, managed through the Prime Minister's Office after the collapse of the Ministry of Information following the October 2023 events, is encountering significant legal challenges. Influencers, consultants, and private companies recruited to promote pro-Israel narratives abroad during the Gaza war have filed lawsuits claiming millions of shekels in unpaid fees. According to reports from Israeli outlet Calcalist, the government bypassed standard tender processes by expanding existing private contracts to channel payments, but many invoices remain unsettled. For instance, Intellect Production and Publishing Group sued for about 1.7 million shekels (around $552,000) related to travel and operations countering pro-Palestine protests at the International Court of Justice. Former government spokesperson Eylon Levy stated he is owed money, with his monthly salary of 41,125 shekels routed through such a company rather than paid directly. Another firm, Spe

item.title: Media Focus on Sexual Scandals vs. Global War Crimes
item.description: The article critiques the disproportionate attention given by the media to alleged sexual misconduct by public figures such as Trump, particularly regarding underage individuals, while largely ignoring the far more severe consequences of geopolitical actions, including wars and military interventions led by the United States. The author argues that these interventions have caused millions of deaths over the past decades, citing bombings, invasions, coups, and sanctions. The piece emphasizes that while sexual misconduct is morally and legally condemnable, it pales in comparison to the large-scale human suffering caused by international conflicts and imperial policies. The author also criticizes the media for selectively framing crimes, presenting minor offenses as sensational and obscuring systemic violence driven by state powers. Additionally, the article touches on current humanitarian crises, such as the impact of sanctions on Cuba, advocating for international cooperation among Span

item.title: Impact of the Strait of Hormuz Closure on Global Oil Markets
