item.description: Researchers at the Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam have officially confirmed that the painting 'Vision of Zacharias in the Temple,' dated 1633, is an authentic early work by Rembrandt van Rijn. The artwork, which had been held in a private collection for the past 65 years, was previously questioned by scholars in the 1960s and largely excluded from Rembrandt's cataloged oeuvre. A comprehensive two-year investigation, prompted by the current owner submitting it for analysis, utilized advanced scientific methods including X-ray fluorescence scans, detailed pigment analysis, brushwork examination, and dendrochronology on the oak panel. These tests revealed that the materials, techniques, and even the wood's tree-ring dating align precisely with Rembrandt's early Amsterdam period around 1633. The painting captures a subtle biblical scene from the Gospel of Luke, showing the high priest Zacharias in the temple, stunned by a divine beam of light announcing that he and his elderly wife will have a s

item.title: Large VA Study Suggests GLP-1 Medications Like Ozempic May Reduce Risk of Multiple Substance Use Disorders and Related Harms
item.description: A new observational study analyzing electronic health records from over 600,000 U.S. veterans with type 2 diabetes, published in The BMJ, indicates that GLP-1 receptor agonist medications—such as semaglutide (Ozempic, Wegovy) and tirzepatide (Mounjaro, Zepbound)—are associated with significant reductions in addiction-related outcomes. These drugs, originally developed for diabetes and obesity, appear to dampen cravings by acting on brain reward pathways involving dopamine. Among patients with existing substance use disorders, those on GLP-1 drugs experienced 50% fewer substance-related deaths, 39% fewer overdoses, 26% fewer drug-related hospitalizations, and 25% fewer suicide attempts over three years, equating to about 12 fewer serious events per 1,000 people. For those without prior addiction, the drugs were linked to an 18% lower risk of developing alcohol use disorder, 25% lower for opioids, and around 20% lower for cocaine and nicotine dependence, preventing roughly 6-7 new diagno

item.title: White House Outlines Iran Military Objectives Without Addressing Support for Iranian People
item.description: In the wake of recent US military action against Iran, the White House has outlined its objectives, but made no mention of supporting the Iranian people or promoting regime change. Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt clarified that the goals of the operation focus on destroying Iran's navy and ballistic missile capabilities, neutralizing regional threats to Americans, and preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. Despite former President Donald Trump's past comments about regime change, the briefing avoided any promises of backing an uprising or providing a mechanism for freedom within Iran. Leavitt stated that 'freedom rests in their hands' without offering specifics on US involvement. Ground troops are not currently planned, and suggestions that Kurdish forces could be used to inspire internal resistance were firmly denied. Leavitt emphasized that 'victory' will be measured by US interests, determined by the president once the objectives are achieved. No timeline for the military

item.title: Sky News and Journalists Receive Top Honors at Royal Television Society Awards
item.description: Sky News has been recognized as News Channel of the Year for the ninth consecutive year at the Royal Television Society Awards. Yalda Hakim, host of the international news program The World With Yalda Hakim, was awarded Presenter of the Year for her extensive reporting from conflict zones such as Israel and Ukraine and interviews with global leaders. Other Sky News journalists also received accolades: Africa correspondent Yousra Elbagir was named Television Journalist of the Year for her reporting on the Sudanese conflict, including investigations into areas targeted during the war. Home news correspondent Mollie Malone earned the Emerging Talent of the Year award for her coverage of UK prison safety issues, the use of drones to smuggle contraband, and the government’s strategies on violence against women and girls. Additionally, John Sparks received the Digital Journalism award for his coverage with Ukrainian troops in regions affected by explosive-carrying drones. Sky News also had m

item.title: Hezbollah’s actions continue to threaten Lebanon’s stability amid regional tensions
item.description: In early 2026, Lebanon faces renewed instability following U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran and the assassination of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Hezbollah, a Shiite paramilitary group and Iranian proxy based in Lebanon, retaliated by firing rockets into northern Israel, prompting Israeli airstrikes on Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, Beirut, and the Bekaa Valley. Although Hezbollah has been significantly weakened since October 2023 due to targeted Israeli strikes on its leadership, it still retains the capacity to provoke conflict and destabilize Lebanon. Attempts at disarmament under a 2024 U.S.-brokered ceasefire have faced setbacks, as Hezbollah refuses to fully disarm while Israel continues its military actions. The Lebanese government recently outlawed Hezbollah’s military activities, a first in the country’s history, but the move has not immediately curbed the group’s operations. The situation heightens sectarian tensions, particularly affecting Lebanon’s Shi

item.title: House Oversight Committee Expands Investigation, Summons Bill Gates and Leon Black to Testify on Epstein Ties
item.description: The U.S. House Oversight Committee has broadened its investigation into the associates of convicted sex trafficker Jeffrey Epstein and his close associate Ghislaine Maxwell. The committee, chaired by Representative James Comer (R-Ky.), has summoned seven additional individuals to testify, including Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Apollo Global Management co-founder Leon Black, and former White House counsel Kathryn Ruemmler. Gates is scheduled for a transcribed interview on May 19 and has confirmed he will cooperate fully, emphasizing that he never witnessed or participated in Epstein’s criminal activities. Documents released by the Department of Justice suggest Gates had personal affairs at the time, but he asserts they did not involve Epstein’s victims. Leon Black, who will testify on May 13, faces allegations in a lawsuit of sexual assault against a minor connected to Epstein, though he previously hired Epstein for personal tax advice. Ruemmler, who resigned from Goldman Sachs due

item.title: Insurance Sector Doubts Effectiveness of Trump’s Plan for Gulf Shipping Security
item.description: US President Donald Trump proposed US-backed insurance coverage and naval escorts for vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz in response to rising tensions with Iran. The move was intended to secure the safe passage of tankers and other maritime trade, particularly energy shipments that account for 20% of the world's oil supply. Despite this announcement on Truth Social, global shipping premiums have surged as underwriters reassess risk. Experts from Lloyd’s of London indicate that the president’s proposals lack clear details, and the presence of US naval escorts could potentially increase the risk to vessels by making them targets for Iranian attacks. The Joint War Committee has expanded its high-risk designation to include the entire Persian Gulf, reflecting a substantial increase in perceived danger. Recent attacks on tankers and oil infrastructure in the Gulf, combined with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, have caused oil and gas prices to spike, along with shipping costs

item.title: Key U.S. and Global Updates: Flood Risks, Legal Cases, and Unusual Rescues
item.description: CNN’s '5 Things PM' highlights several significant stories from March 4, 2026. Rising sea levels are now estimated to be one foot higher than previously thought, prompting concerns about the increasing impact on coastal areas and populations worldwide. In Georgia, Colin Gray, the father of a school shooter, was convicted, marking another case where parents have been held legally accountable for mass shootings, sending a cautionary message to gun owners. Political coverage includes a profile of Jack Schlossberg, JFK’s grandson, as he campaigns for Congress in New York, offering insight into his views on family legacy, political issues, and national landmarks like the Kennedy Center. Health news focuses on erectile dysfunction as a potential early warning for cardiovascular disease in otherwise healthy men, emphasizing the importance of monitoring heart health. Culinary exploration introduces audiences to Korean side dishes beyond the well-known kimchi, encouraging readers to discover di

item.title: Los Angeles Designates Iconic 'Brady Bunch' House as Historic-Cultural Landmark
item.description: The Los Angeles City Council has officially designated the Studio City home featured in the classic television show 'The Brady Bunch' as a historic-cultural monument, ensuring its preservation and cultural recognition. Built in 1959 by architect Harry M. Londelius, the midcentury ranch became famous as the exterior of the Brady family residence, symbolizing the idealized American suburban family life of the late 1960s and early 1970s. HGTV purchased the property in 2018 for $3.5 million and invested $1.9 million to meticulously recreate the show’s interiors in a renovation project documented in the four-part miniseries 'A Very Brady Renovation,' featuring the original cast alongside Drew and Jonathan Scott of 'Property Brothers.' The renovated home now spans over 5,000 square feet, with five bedrooms, five bathrooms, and a hidden second story. After HGTV resold the home at a loss in 2023, it was acquired by historic-home enthusiasts Tina Trahan and Chris Elbrecht. The couple opened the

item.title: Study Shows Valuing Unpaid Household and Care Work Narrows US Income Inequality, But the Equalizing Effect Has Weakened Since 1965
item.description: A recent study published in the March 2026 issue of the Journal of Public Economics examines how including the economic value of unpaid housework and child care alters our understanding of income inequality and living standards in the United States from 1965 to 2018. Researchers valued unpaid domestic work—such as cooking, cleaning, and caregiving—using the typical hourly wage for housekeepers and incorporated it into measures of 'extended income' and 'extended consumption.' The findings reveal that unpaid labor historically acted as a significant equalizer because households across income levels spent similar amounts of time on these tasks. For example, adding the value of extra unpaid hours in lower-income families (often with a stay-at-home spouse) substantially reduced apparent income gaps between high- and low-earning households. However, over the five decades, the time devoted to unpaid work declined sharply, primarily due to women reducing their hours from 37 to 24 per week as m

item.title: Aaron Spencer Wins Republican Primary for Lonoke County Sheriff Amid Pending Murder Trial
item.description: Aaron Spencer, who is facing second-degree murder charges, won the Republican primary for Lonoke County sheriff in Arkansas, receiving 53.5% of the vote. Longtime incumbent John Staley received 26.5%, and third candidate David Bufford received nearly 20%. The primary victory positions Spencer as the frontrunner for the November general election, despite his ongoing trial for the October 2024 fatal shooting of a man accused of sexually abusing his 13-year-old daughter. Spencer has admitted to the killing but pleaded not guilty, arguing he acted to protect his child. His campaign emphasizes restoring accountability and integrity to the sheriff's office. The case has sparked national debate over a parent’s legal limits in protecting their child, drawing both support and criticism from residents. Spencer, 37, served in the Army’s 82nd Airborne Division and has military and private-sector leadership experience, which he cites as preparation for the sheriff role. His trial was postponed earl

item.title: President Trump to Attend Dover Ceremony for Six Service Members Killed by Iranian Drone in Kuwait
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to attend the dignified transfer ceremony for six American service members killed in a deadly Iranian drone strike on a U.S. tactical operations center in a commercial port in Kuwait. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt announced the president's intention during a Wednesday briefing, emphasizing his desire to stand in solidarity with the grieving families. The service members, ranging in age from 20 to 42, were part of Operation Epic Fury, an ongoing U.S. military effort amid escalating tensions with Iran. The attack occurred on Sunday, marking a tragic loss in the broader U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict that has intensified in early 2026. U.S. Central Command identified some of the fallen as Army Reserve soldiers from the 103rd Sustainment Command in Des Moines, Iowa, including a college student from Iowa, a Minnesota mother weeks from returning home, a Nebraska mechanic on his third tour, and a Florida captain with a lifelong calling to serve. These in

item.title: White House Prioritizes Price Stability Amid Iran Conflict, Per Leavitt
item.description: In the midst of escalating tensions in the Iran-US-Israel conflict, White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt emphasized the administration's commitment to maintaining price stability for American consumers. Speaking during ongoing live coverage of the regional crisis, Leavitt highlighted efforts to mitigate economic disruptions caused by surging oil prices following military exchanges between the US, Israel, and Iran. The conflict has rattled global markets, with oil prices spiking due to fears of supply interruptions in the Middle East. Recently, cheap gasoline had provided some relief to households facing high grocery and other costs, but the latest developments threaten to reverse that trend. Leavitt's remarks underscore President Trump's focus on economic resilience, as the administration monitors inflation risks and prepares for potential fallout from an extended war. Wall Street analysts are bracing for broader impacts, including higher energy costs that could fuel inflation and

item.title: Study Shows Shared Experience in Trauma Teams Improves Patient Recovery Times Through Better Coordination
item.description: A new study conducted at UPMC Presbyterian, Pennsylvania's largest major trauma center in Pittsburgh, reveals that trauma resuscitation teams perform better and help patients recover faster when team members have prior shared work experience. Trauma teams are often assembled spontaneously in high-pressure emergency situations involving severe injuries like car crashes, falls, or gunshot wounds, which are leading causes of death, particularly among young people. The research draws on the concept of transactive memory systems (TMS), a behavioral science framework describing a team's collective knowledge of who excels at specific tasks and who to rely on for particular expertise. This shared understanding reduces hesitation, minimizes questions during critical moments, and enables smoother coordination among emergency physicians, surgeons, nurses, anesthesiologists, and respiratory therapists. Analyzing data from multiple trauma cases, the study found that higher levels of shared experien

item.title: James Talarico Wins Texas Democratic Senate Primary, Faces Challenge of Uniting Party
item.description: James Talarico, a 36-year-old Texas state representative and aspiring Presbyterian minister, secured the Democratic nomination for the U.S. Senate after a competitive primary against U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett. Talarico’s campaign focused on a ‘politics of love,’ promoting economic populism and unity rather than combative politics. His approach appealed to voters seeking authenticity and a hopeful message, drawing significant grassroots support and widespread attention from national media. Key moments in the campaign included Talarico’s viral speeches, a high-profile interview with Stephen Colbert, and controversies surrounding remarks allegedly about other candidates, which he denied. Despite a highly organized campaign, he faced challenges from racial divides within the Democratic electorate, with Crockett receiving strong backing from Black voters, particularly in Dallas County. Talarico now faces the task of unifying the party ahead of the general election in November, while Republ

item.title: US Announces Deeper Strikes on Iran as Conflict Expands Regionally with Retaliatory Attacks and Evacuations
item.description: The US and Israel continue intensive military operations against Iran on the fifth day of the conflict, which began with joint airstrikes on February 28 targeting Iranian leadership, nuclear sites, missile facilities, and military infrastructure. US officials, including the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, stated that operations will escalate with progressively deeper strikes into Iranian territory, describing the campaign as still in its early stages. A US submarine sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, and NATO defenses intercepted an Iranian missile heading toward Turkey after passing through Iraq and Syria. Iran has launched retaliatory missile and drone attacks on multiple countries, including Qatar, the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Israel, and others, though the volume of such launches has significantly decreased—down 86% for ballistic missiles and 73% for drones since the first day. Over 1,000 people have been killed in Iran, according to a

item.title: X launches X Money beta with William Shatner auctioning invites for charity
item.description: X, the social media platform formerly known as Twitter, has started offering limited beta access to its new payments service, X Money. Instead of distributing invites traditionally, X owner Elon Musk partnered with actor William Shatner to auction off 42 beta invites. Participants could obtain an invite by donating $1,000 to Shatner's charity supporting children and veterans. The auction was conducted through Shatner's posts on X, and Musk reposted highlights to his own feed. Winners will have early access to the X Money interface, which includes tabs for Account, Rewards, and Activity, with features like sending and requesting money, deposits, and direct deposit options offering up to 6% APY. Funds are held by Cross River Bank and insured up to $250,000 per individual. X Money is part of Musk’s vision to expand X into an “everything app,” alongside X Chat, video, subscriptions, and other services. The service is licensed in over 40 U.S. states and will begin a limited external beta be

item.title: Iran Appeals to UN Following US and Israeli Military Strikes
item.description: On February 28, 2026, as military strikes reportedly targeted Tehran, the Iranian Foreign Ministry issued a statement condemning what it described as 'criminal aggression' by the United States and Israel. The ministry called on the United Nations Security Council to take urgent action, arguing that the attacks violated Article 2, Paragraph 4 of the UN Charter, which prohibits the use of force against sovereign nations. The ministry emphasized Iran's commitment to defending its territory while highlighting its prior efforts to avoid conflict through diplomacy. President Donald Trump characterized the attacks as the beginning of a large-scale operation aimed at overthrowing the Iranian government, intensifying international concern. UN Special Rapporteur Ben Saul condemned the actions on social media, stating that the strikes represented a clear breach of international law and urging governments worldwide to denounce the violations. The incident raises tensions in the Middle East, with p

item.title: Seth Meyers Criticizes Trump for Revealing Military Plans in Iran Conflict
item.description: On March 4, 2026, 'Late Night' host Seth Meyers criticized President Donald Trump for publicly disclosing U.S. military plans during the ongoing conflict in Iran. Speaking about Trump’s remarks to CNN, where the president said the military was 'knocking the crap out of them' and promised that 'the big wave hasn’t even happened,' Meyers warned that such statements could compromise operational security. The comedian emphasized that the commander in chief should be able to keep military strategies confidential and suggested that Trump's excitement over the conflict leads him to overshare sensitive information. Meyers even performed a humorous impression, imagining more unintentional reveals. The broader context involves a U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran, which has resulted in the death of Iranian leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of civilian casualties, and fatalities among American service members from an Iranian drone strike. The escalating war has also disrupted global markets,

item.title: TikTok Confirms It Will Not Implement End-to-End Encryption for Direct Messages Over Safety Concerns
item.description:
TikTok has decided not to introduce end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for direct messages on its platform, according to a report by the BBC. The company stated that implementing E2EE would make it more difficult to protect users, particularly younger audiences, because encrypted messages would prevent law enforcement and TikTok’s internal safety teams from accessing message content when necessary. With end-to-end encryption, only the sender and recipient can read the messages, a feature widely adopted by platforms such as Signal, WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger for one-to-one chats and calls, Apple’s Messages, and Google Messages.
TikTok emphasized that this decision is deliberate and is intended to differentiate its approach from competitors. Instead of E2EE, TikTok uses standard encryption to secure direct messages, similar to the protections used by services like Gmail. The company clarified that only authorized employees can access user messages and only under specific circumstances, such

item.title: Changes in Federal Funding Threaten Permanent Housing Programs for Detroit’s Homeless
item.description: Detroit has seen a worsening homelessness crisis, with around 1,725 people homeless on a single night in 2024, including hundreds of families and over 2,500 children experiencing unstable housing. The city’s homelessness response relies heavily on the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development’s (HUD) Continuum of Care program, which historically allocated 85% to 90% of funding to permanent supportive housing. This model, known as Housing First, provides long-term rental assistance paired with voluntary support services, improving stability, health, and reducing chronic homelessness. In November 2025, HUD announced a shift in funding priorities toward transitional and short-term housing, emphasizing participation in services before or alongside housing placement. Critics argue this 'treatment first' approach undermines evidence showing that stable housing is the foundation for successful recovery and long-term outcomes. Under the proposed changes, Detroit’s permanent supportive h

item.title: Los Angeles City Council Proposes Tax Amnesty for Cannabis Businesses Owing $400 Million in Back Taxes
item.description: More than 500 of Los Angeles' approximately 700 licensed cannabis businesses collectively owe around $400 million in back taxes, penalties, and interest, with the total reaching $417 million by December according to city officials. This includes about $100 million in penalties and $35 million in interest. However, only roughly $150 million is realistically collectible due to statutes of limitations and defunct businesses. The Los Angeles City Council unanimously voted to direct the Office of Finance to draft an amnesty program allowing businesses to pay principal taxes owed within three years, forgiving late fees and interest. Projections indicate that if about half of eligible businesses participate, the city could collect approximately $30 million in back taxes while waiving around $25 million in penalties. Of the collected revenue, 20% would support the general fund and finance office, 40% would fund enforcement against illegal cannabis operations by the LAPD and city attorney, and

item.title: Congress’s Struggle to Limit Presidential War Powers Over Time
item.description: Article 1 of the U.S. Constitution grants Congress the authority to declare war, yet modern presidents frequently claim Article 2 allows them to act militarily without prior congressional approval. Over decades, Congress has often responded weakly to such actions, from interventions in Panama to Libya. A notable exception was the War Powers Resolution of 1973, passed in response to President Richard Nixon's expansion of the Vietnam War into Cambodia. Despite Nixon's veto, Congress overrode it with a two-thirds majority, asserting its constitutional power. The resolution established rules for presidential military action, including a 60-day limit on hostilities without congressional approval, reporting requirements, and circumstances under which presidents may act independently. Since then, presidents from both parties have largely respected the reporting requirements but rarely sought explicit approval, often exploiting the 60-to-90-day window. Authorizations for Use of Military Force,

item.title: Israeli F-35I Shoots Down Iranian Yak-130 Fighter Jet Over Tehran in Historic First Air-to-Air Kill
item.description: On March 4, 2026, the Israeli military announced that one of its F-35I 'Adir' stealth fighter jets successfully shot down an Iranian Air Force Yak-130 aircraft over the skies of Tehran. This event marks the first time an F-35 has downed a manned fighter jet in combat, as well as a rare instance of air-to-air engagement for the Israeli Air Force in recent decades. Maj. Gen. Tomer Bar, commander of the Israeli Air Force, described the shootdown as a historic milestone that demonstrates the strength and determination of Israeli pilots amid the ongoing conflict. The F-35I, Israel's customized variant of the U.S.-made F-35 Lightning II, has been in service with Israel since 2016. The downed aircraft, a Russian-made Yak-130, is primarily a two-seat advanced trainer and light attack jet that Iran began receiving in 2023. The incident occurred as broader Israeli and U.S. operations continued against Iranian targets following escalations in the region, including prior strikes on leadership and

item.title: Michelin-Starred Chef Jean Delport Shares Insights on Great British Menu, Michelin Pressures, Customer Habits, and British Cuisine
item.description: In a monthly Cheap Eats interview with Sky News, Michelin-starred chef Jean Delport of Restaurant Interlude in West Sussex discusses his experience competing on last year's Great British Menu. He describes the intense preparation, often practicing late into the night while managing his restaurant, calling it an all-consuming yet rewarding challenge. His strong performance on the show significantly boosted bookings and revitalized his team, putting the restaurant on the national map and attracting diners from across the UK. Delport reflects on the ongoing pressure of maintaining a Michelin star, describing it as both exhilarating and exhausting due to the constant need to uphold and exceed standards. He advises guests to adjust tips based on service quality and to voice complaints politely during the meal for immediate resolution, criticizing the habit of leaving without feedback as unhelpful. A major pet peeve is customers mislabeling personal dislikes as allergies, which restricts kit

item.title: Offshore Floating Data Centers Powered by Wind Energy Could Address AI Energy Challenges
item.description: With the growing energy demands of AI data centers, some innovators have considered placing servers in space to access continuous solar power. However, a startup called Aikido is pursuing a more practical approach by situating data centers offshore. This year, Aikido plans to deploy a 100-kilowatt demonstration data center submerged within the floating pods of an offshore wind turbine off the coast of Norway. If successful, a larger-scale project is expected by 2028 near the UK, featuring a 15–18 megawatt turbine powering a 10–12 megawatt data center. Offshore placement provides consistent wind power, proximity to energy generation, and simplified cooling through cold seawater. It also mitigates local opposition from communities concerned about noise and pollution. Nevertheless, challenges include the corrosive nature of seawater and the need for secure, stationary placement of submerged equipment. While not a new idea—Microsoft conducted a submerged data center experiment off Scotland

item.title: n8n: Open-Source Workflow Automation Platform with AI and 400+ Integrations
item.description: n8n is a versatile workflow automation platform designed for technical teams that need both flexibility and speed. Combining a visual interface with the option to write custom JavaScript or Python code, it allows users to automate complex tasks without losing full control over their data and deployments. The platform supports over 400 integrations, includes native AI capabilities, and enables the creation of AI-driven workflows using LangChain with personalized data and models. Users can self-host n8n under its fair-code license or opt for a cloud deployment. Enterprise features include advanced permissions, single sign-on (SSO), and air-gapped deployments, making it suitable for large organizations. With a strong community, n8n provides 900+ prebuilt workflow templates and extensive documentation to facilitate quick adoption. Installation is straightforward via Node.js with 'npx n8n' or through Docker. The project name, n8n, is derived from 'nodemation,' combining 'node' from Node.js

item.title: Researchers Study Encounters with Nonhuman Entities During DMT Experiences
item.description: A new research initiative is exploring the experiences of people who encounter nonhuman entities while under the influence of DMT, a powerful psychedelic. The project, called Eleusis, is launching a retreat on the Caribbean island of Bequia, combining therapeutic support with scientific study. Participants receive DMT through an IV infusion, which allows for controlled, extended sessions known as DMTx, providing a longer and more manageable psychedelic experience compared to traditional vaping or ayahuasca ceremonies. The research aims to investigate whether perceived encounters with highly intelligent, alien-like entities are purely hallucinatory or represent an untapped dimension of consciousness. While proponents like neurobiologist Andrew Gallimore argue that these entities could exist independently of the human mind, other experts, such as Robin Carhart-Harris and Susan Blackmore, suggest these experiences are the brain’s interpretation of highly complex sensory input. Eleusis com

item.title: Apple introduces iPhone 17e with higher base storage starting at $599
item.description: Apple has officially unveiled the iPhone 17e, its more affordable smartphone model, starting at $599 with 256GB of storage. The launch reflects Apple's strategy to maintain market share amid rising memory chip prices and global supply constraints. By providing more storage at the same price point as previous models, Apple is effectively offering added value to consumers without increasing costs. The iPhone 17e features the latest A19 chip built on 3-nanometer technology, delivering improved performance for entry-level users. Additionally, it includes the new C1X modem for faster cellular speeds and better energy efficiency, as well as MagSafe support for the first time in the “e” series, allowing compatibility with Apple’s magnetic accessories and wireless charging up to 15 watts. Pre-orders will be available in over 70 countries starting March 4 at 6:15 a.m. PT, with sales beginning March 11. Alongside the iPhone 17e, Apple introduced a redesigned iPad Air powered by the M4 chip, with

item.title: Gavin Newsom Shares Unusual Conversation With Donald Trump Before National Guard Deployment
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom recounted his experiences speaking with former President Donald Trump during an appearance on Jimmy Kimmel’s late-night show, highlighting a particularly unusual phone call. Newsom, who is considered a potential Democratic presidential candidate for 2028, shared details of the call that occurred the night before Trump mobilized the National Guard to Los Angeles. Contrary to Trump’s later claim that he had 'read the riot act' to Newsom, the governor described the conversation as light-hearted and bizarre. Trump reportedly referred to Newsom with the nickname 'Newscum,' discussed merchandise like MAGA hats, and engaged in casual, almost nonsensical banter. Newsom said the dialogue included Trump asking about hat sales and joking in a manner more reminiscent of a schoolyard exchange than a serious political conversation. Despite the unusual tone of the call, the National Guard deployment proceeded shortly afterward. Newsom described these interactions as t

item.title: Five Years Using a Brain-Computer Interface: Rodney Gorham’s Experience with ALS
item.description: Rodney Gorham, a 65-year-old man with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), has lived with a Synchron brain-computer interface for five years, making him the longest user of this experimental technology. The implant, called the Stentrode, is a mesh tube placed via the jugular vein to the motor cortex, allowing Gorham to control digital devices using only his thoughts. Over time, he progressed from simple single clicks to multi-clicks, sliding controls, and full 2D cursor movement. He has used the interface to operate smart home devices, iPhones, iPads, and even a robotic vacuum, contributing directly to Apple’s Switch Control accessibility feature. Gorham’s involvement has helped Synchron refine both hardware and software, including removing wires in its second-generation system to make it easier for caregivers to use. While Gorham’s ALS has progressed, requiring more concentration and causing fatigue, his experience raises broader questions about the long-term usability of brain-comput

item.title: Current Tensions Between the US and UK Amid Iran and Epstein Controversies
item.description: The Sky News podcast highlights growing tensions between the United States and the United Kingdom, particularly focusing on the political dynamic between former President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The program explores Trump's ongoing conflict with Iran and how it has created diplomatic strains, potentially impacting the traditional US-UK special relationship. Senior US officials provide insight into why the United States remains involved in Iran, giving listeners a detailed briefing on current geopolitical motivations. The episode also addresses the ongoing issues surrounding the Jeffrey Epstein files, noting that many documents have been removed from public access, leaving questions about transparency and accountability. Overall, the podcast examines how these high-profile disputes—ranging from international policy to sensitive legal matters—could affect bilateral relations, media coverage, and public perception, offering analysis of the evolving political landscap

item.title: Israeli Strike in Qom Leaves Iranian Clerics Vulnerable Amid Khamenei Succession Uncertainty
item.description: An Israeli airstrike targeted a meeting location in Qom, Iran, where senior clerics of the Assembly of Experts were reportedly planning to discuss succession plans following the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Defense analysts claim the strike exposed the clerics, leaving Iran's leadership feeling insecure and under constant surveillance. It is unclear how many of the 88 members were present at the time. This incident follows an earlier strike on Khamenei's Tehran compound, signaling continued military pressure. Analysts suggest that the Iranian regime is now vulnerable and potentially facing internal instability, with fears of uprisings from within. The strikes are part of a broader campaign, Operation Epic Fury, in which U.S. forces have targeted over 1,700 Iranian military and command sites in the first 72 hours. Targets include IRGC headquarters, air defense systems, and missile sites, aiming to neutralize imminent threats and limit Iran’s military capabilities. Exp

item.title: Lessons from the 1953 CIA Regime Change in Iran and Trump's Lack of a Clear Strategy
item.description: The article compares the U.S. bombing of Iran in 2026 with the CIA's covert operation in 1953 that successfully ousted Iran's Prime Minister Mohammad Mossadegh. The Trump administration's 2026 military actions in Iran have been criticized for lacking clear objectives or a coherent strategy. This analysis reflects on how the 1953 CIA operation, which included propaganda, false flag incidents, and political maneuvering, achieved its aim of strengthening the Shah's regime. The operation contrasts with Trump’s approach, which has not provided clear guidance on achieving regime change. The piece suggests that the lessons from the 1953 operation might offer insights into how political change in Iran could still be possible, though the current context with Israel's involvement and the widespread use of social media makes this approach more challenging today. Despite the complexities, the article emphasizes that opposition forces in Iran deserve support to organize effectively against their go

item.title: Over a Ton of Methamphetamine Seized in Major Northern California Drug Bust
item.description: A recent operation in Northern California led to one of the largest domestic drug seizures in U.S. history, uncovering over a ton of methamphetamine. The operation, dubbed 'Operation Trash Panda,' took place on February 27, 2026, and was executed by the Sacramento County Sheriff’s Office and the Calaveras County Sheriff’s Office, with over 100 law enforcement agents involved. Authorities discovered three key locations connected to the methamphetamine trade. The first site was an active meth lab, the second contained the equipment for manufacturing but was not yet operational, and the third was a storage and distribution center for illicit drugs. In total, law enforcement agents seized over 2,700 pounds of methamphetamine, worth approximately $4 million. They also recovered 1,900 marijuana plants and over 100 pounds of processed marijuana. The investigation, which began last October, was triggered after authorities found byproducts from illegal drug labs in an illegal dumping site in Ca

item.title: DNC Cancels Midterm Convention Due to Fundraising Challenges
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) has announced that it will not be hosting a midterm convention, citing significant fundraising difficulties as the main reason. The decision was made after feedback from within the party, suggesting that resources should instead be focused on key campaign work in critical states. The DNC is currently facing a financial shortfall, with the Republican National Committee (RNC) holding a significant cash advantage of $100 million going into 2026. This situation prompted the DNC to take out a $15 million loan in late 2025 for the Virginia and New Jersey elections, raising concerns about the party's financial health. In addition to the cancellation of the midterm convention, the DNC has announced its plans for the 2028 presidential convention, scheduled from August 7 to 10, 2028, with Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Denver, and Philadelphia under consideration as potential host cities. DNC Chair Ken Martin emphasized that the committee was prioritizing campa

item.title: Third Way Centrists Discuss 2028 Strategy Amid Iran War Divisions
item.description: Centrist Democrats gathered at a Third Way conference to strategize for the 2028 presidential election, focusing initially on domestic issues like affordability and elevating moderate voices online. The event, attended by consultants and strategists linked to potential candidates such as Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear, Arizona Sen. Mark Kelly, and California Gov. Gavin Newsom, quickly pivoted to the ongoing war with Iran following President Trump's military actions. Third Way President Jon Cowan opened by acknowledging skepticism about the conflict, criticizing Trump's credibility while celebrating the potential downfall of Iran's regime. The discussion revealed deepening fractures within the Democratic Party over foreign policy. Progressives like Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Sen. Bernie Sanders demanded an immediate end to the war, while moderates, including House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, adopted a more measured stance, condemning Trump for byp

item.title: Exiled Iranian Opposes Both US-Israeli Attack and Iranian Regime
item.description: In an interview with Middle East Eye, Behrouz Farahani, a political analyst in exile and critic of the Islamic Republic of Iran, strongly condemned the US and Israeli military actions against Iran. Farahani emphasized that he and other Iranian opposition figures who oppose the war are equally opposed to both sides. He described the conflict as a struggle between the US and Israel on one side and the reactionary Iranian regime on the other. Farahani argued that such a war will only lead to suffering for the Iranian people, rather than progress or improvements in their daily lives. He also pointed out that US President Trump’s financial interests are a key driver of the military aggression, suggesting that the US's true motivations are economic rather than humanitarian. Importantly, Farahani clarified that while the US may be in conflict with Iran, this does not mean that the Islamic Republic represents a progressive or anti-imperialist force. His comments reflect widespread criticism of

item.title: Bears center Drew Dalman unexpectedly retires at 27 after stellar career
item.description: Drew Dalman, the Pro Bowl center for the Chicago Bears, has announced his retirement at just 27 years old, shocking the NFL community. Dalman had a standout career, playing five seasons in the league. Last year, he signed a 3-year, $42 million contract with the Bears and was a key figure in the team’s offensive line overhaul. He started all 17 games during the 2025 season, earning his first Pro Bowl selection and helping the Bears secure the NFC North title with a strong offensive performance. The Bears’ revamped line contributed significantly to the team’s success, including improved protection for quarterback Caleb Williams and a productive rushing attack led by D’Andre Swift and Kyle Monangai. Despite the impressive numbers, Dalman decided to retire early, leaving the Bears to find a replacement either through free agency or the upcoming draft. The team is also facing a key offseason challenge with left tackle Ozzy Trapilo’s recent injury. With free agency starting on March 11, the

item.title: CIA Collaborating with Kurdish Forces to Support Uprising Against Iranian Regime
item.description: The CIA is reportedly working to arm Kurdish forces in Iraq with the aim of sparking a popular uprising in Iran. Sources close to the situation indicate that the Trump administration is engaged in discussions with Kurdish leaders and Iranian opposition groups. These Kurdish forces, operating along the Iraq-Iran border, are prepared for a possible ground operation in Western Iran. There is hope that by supporting Kurdish groups, the U.S. can inspire Iranians to rise against the government. The U.S. is also coordinating with Israeli forces to prepare for the potential movement of Kurdish fighters into northwest Iran. However, concerns remain about the effectiveness of Kurdish forces, given their limited resources and influence. Furthermore, U.S. officials are wary of the complexities of working with Kurdish groups, who have a history of political fragmentation and distrust toward U.S. support. While there is a strategic effort to weaken Iran, experts caution that arming the Kurds could c

item.title: US Consulate in Dubai Hit by Suspected Iranian Drone Amid Growing Middle East Tensions
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East escalate as new military strikes and retaliations continue. The US consulate in Dubai was hit by a suspected Iranian drone, marking a significant escalation. Meanwhile, President Donald Trump has ordered new military strikes targeting Iranian leadership after US and Israeli forces took out key military installations in Iran. Israel also attacked a compound linked to the group responsible for electing Iran's next Supreme Leader. The conflict has led to a sharp rise in oil prices, with the Strait of Hormuz seeing a de facto shutdown as oil shipments are delayed. Additionally, American citizens in the Gulf are increasingly frustrated with the US government's response, with some choosing to stay despite the escalating danger. The situation remains volatile, with diplomatic talks currently suspended as military action takes precedence. Iran has suffered significant casualties, with over 780 deaths reported from the bombing campaign. The international community is

item.title: Iran Conflict Challenges UK's Inflation Forecast
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran has dramatically impacted global energy markets, with wholesale gas prices in the UK surging. This rise is particularly alarming because gas prices are a key determinant of electricity, industrial production, and food costs in Britain. Despite the rapid escalation in gas prices over the past 48 hours, which has surpassed previous energy crises, the UK government's spring budget forecast for low inflation, based on outdated assumptions, is now seriously in question. The Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR) had predicted a gradual decrease in energy prices, but the unforeseen escalation in the Middle East has turned these predictions upside down. The effects of the war, particularly attacks on oil and gas facilities by Iranian drones, are expected to push household bills higher. While it is uncertain how long the conflict will last or how much gas prices could climb, its immediate impact is already undermining the UK's economic recovery from previous energy pric

item.title: S&P 500 Rebounds as Investors Buy the Dip Following U.S.-Iran Conflict
item.description: The S&P 500 ended the day largely unchanged, recovering from earlier sharp declines, as traders took advantage of the dip following recent U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran. The market's recovery was driven by a drop in U.S. oil prices from their session highs, alleviating fears over the conflict's impact on the economy. Key tech stocks, such as Nvidia and Microsoft, saw gains, signaling confidence that companies with strong cash positions could weather the geopolitical storm. Despite these tensions, historical patterns show that stock markets often rebound after geopolitical crises. The Dow Jones Industrial Average ended the day down 0.15%, while the Nasdaq Composite rose by 0.36%. Investors are also closely monitoring the potential impact of the war on global oil supply, especially as Iran, a major oil producer, has threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz. Despite the geopolitical risks, investors are maintaining a cautiously optimistic view, with defense and energy stocks benefit

item.title: Experts Weigh In on Possible Causes of Trump's Neck Rash
item.description: On March 2, 2026, an angry rash on President Donald Trump’s neck sparked public speculation about his health. The White House confirmed that Trump is using a common skin cream as a preventative treatment, but did not specify the exact nature of the condition. Experts, including Dr. Elizabeth Bahar Houshmand and Dr. Anthony Rossi, weighed in on the possible causes of the rash. They suggested it could be the result of actinic keratoses, a sun-induced skin condition often referred to as 'pre-cancerous' lesions, commonly seen in people with extensive sun exposure. Since Trump is known to be an avid golfer, it is plausible that this is a reaction to prolonged UV exposure. Dr. Rossi also mentioned that it could be due to topical chemotherapy creams, such as 5-fluorouracil or imiquimod, which are used to treat such lesions. Both dermatologists emphasized that this is merely speculation, as they have not examined Trump directly. Without more details from the White House, the cause of the rash

item.title: KSI Invests in Dagenham & Redbridge with Goal to Reach Premier League
item.description: YouTuber and entrepreneur KSI has purchased a minority stake in Dagenham & Redbridge Football Club, a team currently struggling in the sixth tier of English football. The club, which has faced relegation to the National League South, announced changes in its management, including the departure of managing director Stephen Thompson. KSI expressed his excitement about the new venture and his ambitions for the club, stating that he hopes to lead them back to their 'glory days' and eventually push for promotion to the Premier League, although he emphasized the long-term nature of the goal. The 32-year-old, whose real name is Olajide Olatunji, rose to fame as a YouTube personality and is now involved in various ventures, including music and television. Dagenham & Redbridge, which was formed in 1992, has had a turbulent history, with their most successful period being a brief stay in League One during the 2009-2010 season. The club's most recent setback came when they were relegated from the

item.title: Carrie Underwood responds to 'American Idol' boos amid political speculation
item.description: Carrie Underwood, the country music superstar and judge on ‘American Idol,’ has remained unapologetic despite receiving boos from the audience during a recent episode. The backlash occurred after she delivered pointed critiques to contestants. While some viewers initially believed the boos were due to her judging, others quickly tied the reaction to her perceived political views. Underwood, known for her conservative leanings and support of former President Trump, shrugged off the criticism, stating, 'Boo me. I don’t care.' Many of her supporters defended her, praising her for standing firm against Hollywood’s left-leaning culture. The incident also led to speculation about her performance at President Trump’s 2025 inauguration, where she sang ‘America the Beautiful.’ Despite the mixed reactions, Underwood has continued to assert that she avoids partisanship and simply wants to focus on her music and career. The controversy, however, continues to spark debates about the intersection of

item.title: James Webb Telescope Discovers Distant Jellyfish Galaxy with Star Formation
item.description: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have identified the most distant jellyfish galaxy ever observed. This galaxy, located 8.5 billion light-years away, exhibits long, flowing trails of gas and newly formed stars, which is characteristic of jellyfish galaxies. These unusual galaxies move through galaxy clusters filled with hot gas, which causes their gas to be stripped off, forming long, tentacle-like trails. This phenomenon is called ram-pressure stripping. The galaxy was found in the COSMOS field, a region that has been extensively studied by multiple telescopes. This discovery offers a rare glimpse into the early universe, revealing a more violent and turbulent environment than previously thought. The discovery challenges earlier assumptions that galaxy clusters at this time were still forming and that such stripping was uncommon. The presence of young stars forming in the stripped gas also offers valuable insight into galaxy evolution. Researchers hope to observe

item.title: Resilient Bacterium Survives Martian Impact Forces, Suggesting Life Could Transfer Between Planets
item.description: A groundbreaking experiment has shown that the famously resilient bacterium Deinococcus radiodurans can survive the crushing forces from a massive asteroid impact on Mars. This discovery opens up the possibility that life could be transferred from one planet to another through asteroid impacts. In the study, researchers subjected the bacteria to extreme pressures—up to 30,000 times that of Earth's atmosphere—by sandwiching it between steel plates and using a third plate to create intense force. Despite the harsh conditions, around 60% of the bacteria survived. The study's authors found that the bacterium was able to repair cellular damage and even survive the crushing pressures that could be encountered when microbes are ejected into space by a massive impact. These findings suggest that life on Mars could survive the harsh conditions of an asteroid impact and potentially spread across space, furthering the hypothesis of interplanetary life transfer. The research is a significant step

item.title: Texas Senate race pits Ken Paxton against John Cornyn in a high‑stakes GOP primary runoff
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton and longtime U.S. Senator John Cornyn are advancing to a May 26 primary runoff for the Republican nomination in Texas’s Senate race, setting up a bitter intra‑party showdown that could reshape the state’s political landscape. The article details how the three‑way Republican contest, which also included Rep. Wesley Hunt, narrowed to a head‑to‑head battle between Cornyn, a four‑term incumbent associated with the GOP establishment, and Paxton, a Trump‑aligned MAGA figure with a history of legal and ethical controversies. Polling shows Paxton with a modest lead, but Cornyn’s allies argue his experience and national network make him better positioned to win both the runoff and a fall general election against a top‑tier Democrat. National Republicans are deeply divided over the race, with some warning a Paxton victory could open the door to Democrats in a traditionally red state, while grassroots MAGA activists see his candidacy as a chance to overthrow the

item.title: How Democratic Senate primaries are being reshaped by candidates’ positions on Israel and Gaza
item.description: The article examines how Democratic candidates’ relationships with Israel, and their responses to the Gaza war, have become central fault lines in high‑profile primary contests across the United States. In Illinois’ Senate primary, Rep. Robin Kelly challenges Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton over a 2019 meeting Stratton held with former Israeli Foreign Minister Tzipi Livni, arguing that this encounter raises questions about Stratton’s leadership and judgment. Kelly presents her criticism as part of a broader progressive record, emphasizing that she has condemned what happened in Gaza as genocide, rejected campaign money from the pro‑Israel lobby group AIPAC, and refused funds from companies tied to ICE. Her stance, however, has evolved: after entering the Senate race in May, she shifted away from earlier, closer ties to pro‑Israel interests, including having previously accepted AIPAC donations and traveled repeatedly to Israel on congressional trips, before announcing in January that she woul

item.title: Advanced Plasma Sensors Could Be Key to Unlocking Commercial Fusion Power
item.description: Fusion energy has the potential to be a significant source of clean energy, but researchers face challenges in measuring the extreme and fast-moving plasmas necessary for fusion reactions. A new report, backed by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), stresses the need for investment in advanced diagnostic tools—sensors that track plasma's temperature, density, and behavior inside fusion devices. These sensors are crucial to ensuring the safe and efficient operation of fusion reactors. The DOE's 2024 workshop gathered experts from universities, national laboratories, and private industries to identify priority areas for improving plasma measurement technologies. Key recommendations include developing more durable sensors for extreme radiation environments, improving AI and machine learning tools to speed up measurement system design, and fostering a strong workforce to support the growing field. The workshop's findings also support the DOE's roadmap for advancing fusion power, which aims

item.title: How the Iran Conflict is Affecting UK Energy Prices and Consumer Bills
item.description: The ongoing Iran conflict is having significant economic ramifications that are already pushing up global oil and gas prices. The Strait of Hormuz, a key oil trade route, has been affected by Iranian military threats, causing insurance issues for shipping vessels. As a result, oil prices have jumped 18%, driving up wholesale petrol and diesel prices. While UK households may not immediately feel the sting due to a recent energy price cap, the long-term impact could be significant, particularly if the conflict persists. Gas prices have surged over 93% since the war began, and wholesale gas prices have more than doubled in recent days. This will likely lead to higher energy costs for both consumers and businesses in the UK, with the latter facing the highest energy prices in the developed world. Although there's no immediate rise in domestic bills due to the current price cap, the situation could worsen after June. The war has reignited debates about the UK's energy policy, with some advo

item.title: Shortage of Public Defenders Results in Hundreds of Criminal Case Dismissals in Oregon and Across the U.S.
item.description: Oregon's Supreme Court recently ruled that more than 1,400 criminal cases must be dismissed due to a severe shortage of public defenders. This issue isn't isolated to Oregon; across the U.S., many states face similar shortages. For example, Pennsylvania is short by about 30% of the public defenders it needs, New Mexico needs 67% more, and Kansas requires nearly three times as many. The problem of not having enough defense attorneys affects not only the defendants but the entire criminal justice system, leading to longer jail times, wrongful convictions, delays in prosecuting cases, and rising costs. The Sixth Amendment guarantees the right to an attorney, and the U.S. Supreme Court's 1963 Gideon v. Wainwright decision reinforced this. However, the pool of attorneys available to fulfill this role is shrinking, especially in rural areas. Financial incentives like loan forgiveness are one potential solution, but they are not enough. Long-term solutions may involve building better recruitm

item.title: Tech Entrepreneur Ethan Agarwal Challenges Ro Khanna for California's 17th Congressional District
item.description: Ethan Agarwal, a 40-year-old tech entrepreneur, has announced his candidacy for California’s 17th Congressional District, challenging incumbent Representative Ro Khanna. Agarwal, who has no political background, was previously exploring a run for governor of California but pivoted to the congressional race after Khanna endorsed a wealth tax proposal. This has fueled backlash from Silicon Valley’s billionaire class, many of whom support Agarwal's campaign. Khanna, a Democrat, has faced criticism for his stance on the wealth tax and his high-profile stock trading activities, which Agarwal highlights in his campaign. Agarwal's platform includes proposals to increase taxes on loans taken out against assets and higher property taxes for investment properties. He also calls for a ban on stock trading for Congress members, term limits, and banning corporate PAC money. Agarwal, who is supported by figures such as Garry Tan and Stanley Tang, emphasizes that he is focused on representing the loc

item.title: March 2026 Netflix Releases: New Shows, Movies, and BTS Comeback
item.description: Netflix’s March 2026 lineup is packed with exciting content, including the much-anticipated ‘Peaky Blinders’ sequel, a new experimental dating show, and a horror miniseries from the creators of ‘Stranger Things.’ Among the highlights are new seasons of ‘Virgin River,’ ‘Beauty in Black,’ and the live-action ‘One Piece.’ Additionally, the platform is set to release the twisted adaptation of Julia May Jonas’ novel ‘Vladimir,’ starring Rachel Weisz and Leo Woodall. Other notable releases include a documentary on Lamar Odom, a BTS comeback concert, and a documentary on ultra-masculinity. The month will also feature action-packed films like ‘War Machine’ and the long-awaited ‘Peaky Blinders’ film, ‘The Immortal Man.’ Fans of all genres can look forward to an eventful month on the streaming platform with new episodes, documentaries, and live performances. Check out Netflix’s full March 2026 catalog for a complete guide to everything coming to the service.

item.title: Reporting From Iran Amid Internet Blackouts: How Journalists Are Adapting
item.description: After Israeli and American strikes killed senior Iranian officials in Tehran, the Iranian government imposed a near-total internet blackout. This move has severely impacted journalists and activists inside the country, forcing them to find alternative methods of communication. Many have turned to satellite links, encrypted apps, and smuggled footage to continue reporting to the outside world. Tehran-based journalist Mostafa Zadeh shares his experience, stating that internet blackouts are common during times of unrest or government crackdowns. Despite access to tools like Starlink, journalists face the constant risk of arrest for trying to circumvent the government-imposed restrictions. Some, like Erfan Khorshidi, have taken a more proactive approach by smuggling Starlink terminals to local dissidents to enable real-time communication. However, this comes with significant risks, as authorities now tightly monitor internet connections and have introduced harsher espionage laws. Satellite

item.title: ChatGPT Sees Massive Spike in Uninstalls Following Department of Defense Deal
item.description: The mobile app uninstalls of ChatGPT saw a staggering 295% jump on February 28, 2026, in the U.S., following news of OpenAI's partnership with the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD). This spike marked a major contrast to ChatGPT's usual uninstall rate of 9% day-over-day. The deal raised concerns among consumers, especially after the DoD rebranded as the Department of War under the Trump administration. OpenAI’s competitor, Anthropic, also witnessed a surge, with its AI app Claude experiencing a 37% increase in downloads the day before and a 51% rise the day after the DoD announcement. In response, Anthropic made it clear it would not collaborate with the DoD, citing concerns over AI being used for surveillance and autonomous weaponry. The backlash against ChatGPT was further reflected in a significant uptick in negative 1-star reviews, which jumped by 775% on February 28. Meanwhile, Claude reached No. 1 on the U.S. App Store, marking a sharp rise in its rankings. The changes in downloads

item.title: How Constant War Updates Affect Mental Health and Social Media Use
item.description: Recent missile strikes in the Persian Gulf, following US-Israel attacks in Iran, have led to widespread doomscrolling, where users compulsively consume negative news through social media feeds. This behavior is amplified by algorithm-driven platforms that continuously deliver alerts, videos, and updates, creating a feedback loop of threat monitoring. Experts note that humans are naturally wired to prioritize danger, making negative news particularly difficult to ignore. Cognitive and media psychologists warn that prolonged exposure to crisis content can increase anxiety, stress, rumination, and even resemble indirect trauma exposure. The article highlights that doomscrolling is distinct from 'dopamine scrolling,' as it focuses on threat-related material rather than stimulation. Researchers emphasize that this habit is reinforced by unpredictable feeds, notifications, and emotionally charged content, which keeps the brain's threat-detection system activated. Suggested strategies to redu

item.title: Pronto expands online domestic services in India with rapid growth and $100M valuation
item.description: Bengaluru-based startup Pronto is rapidly transforming India's largely informal domestic services market by moving it online. Founded less than a year ago, Pronto recently raised $25 million in a Series B funding round led by Epiq Capital, boosting its valuation to $100 million. Existing investors including Glade Brook Capital, General Catalyst, and Bain Capital Ventures participated, bringing total funding to around $40 million. The platform provides trained and background-verified professionals for daily chores such as sweeping, mopping, and utensil cleaning, dispatching workers within about 10 minutes in its key micromarkets. With 18,000 daily bookings currently, Pronto aims to reach 70,000 by June. The company has expanded from one city to ten, covering over 150 micromarkets, though half of its activity still comes from the National Capital Region. The workforce is predominantly female, with 4,500 active professionals earning a median monthly income of ₹23,000 to ₹25,000. Monthly r

item.title: Alan Cumming Criticizes BAFTAs Broadcast Handling of Racial Slur Incident Involving Tourette's Campaigner
item.description: Actor and host Alan Cumming, who presented the 2026 BAFTA Film Awards, issued a strongly worded statement one week after the ceremony, calling the event a 'trauma triggering shitshow.' The controversy stemmed from an incident where John Davidson, a campaigner with Tourette's syndrome attending to celebrate the nominated film about his life titled 'I Swear,' involuntarily shouted a racial slur (the N-word) during a presentation by Black actors Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo from the film 'Sinners.' The BBC broadcast captured the moment live and failed to edit it out promptly, leaving the unedited version available on BBC iPlayer for over 12 hours before removal, sparking widespread global discussion and outrage. Cumming expressed deep regret for the pain caused to Black viewers hearing the slur amplified worldwide and for the Tourette's community facing renewed stigma and misunderstanding about the condition. He highlighted that the only potential positive outcome was increased awar

item.title: Spain Refuses U.S. Access to Military Bases for Iran Airstrikes
item.description: Spain has opposed the use of its military bases by the U.S. for attacks on Iran, following weekend airstrikes coordinated by the U.S. and Israel. The Spanish Foreign Minister, Jose Manuel Albares, made it clear that bases like Rota and Moron, which are shared by the U.S. and Spain, would not be used for any military actions against Iran, condemning the U.S.-Israeli operations. In contrast, the UK initially resisted but later allowed the use of its bases for ‘collective self-defense,’ under the authorization of Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Despite the U.S. and Israel's ongoing operations, Spain’s government stands firm in its stance that any military activity on its soil must align with international law and existing agreements with the U.S. The refusal adds to Spain’s growing divergence from U.S. policies in the region, particularly under the leadership of Socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. Some aircraft, including aerial refueling tankers, have already left Spanish bases, reporte

item.title: Surprising Biodiversity Found in the Atacama Desert's Soil Ecosystems
item.description: A recent study led by the University of Cologne has revealed that the Atacama Desert, one of the driest places on Earth, is home to diverse and resilient life beneath its surface. Researchers focused on tiny soil-dwelling nematodes, discovering that these organisms thrive despite the harsh environment of high salt content, extreme temperature variations, and almost no rainfall. The research team analyzed various regions of the desert, finding that biodiversity was greater in areas with more moisture, and that altitude played a significant role in determining which species could survive. In particular, nematodes in higher elevations were more likely to reproduce asexually, suggesting this method of reproduction may be an advantage in extreme conditions. This discovery not only highlights the unexpected richness of life in arid regions but also signals potential ecological vulnerabilities. With increasing global aridity, the study's findings may have implications for understanding how de

item.title: Netanyahu: US and Israel’s strikes on Iran aim to end regime, not start a prolonged war
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu reassured the public that the US and Israel's strikes against Iran would not escalate into an endless conflict. Speaking in an exclusive interview with Fox News, Netanyahu emphasized that the Iranian regime, now at its weakest point in decades, would soon be toppled. He argued that the targeted military actions, part of 'Operation Epic Fury,' were necessary to prevent Iran from further advancing its nuclear and ballistic missile programs. Netanyahu also highlighted the potential for regional peace, noting that the downfall of Iran’s regime could lead to peace treaties with other Middle Eastern nations, particularly Saudi Arabia. The Prime Minister stressed that this intervention is not about dragging the US into a protracted war but about securing long-term peace in the region, echoing successes like the Abraham Accords brokered by former President Trump. Netanyahu dismissed claims that Israel had pushed the US into this conflict, asserting tha

item.title: Iran's Escalation Forces Gulf States into Conflict They Tried to Avoid
item.description: The Gulf nations, historically cautious of becoming embroiled in the Middle East conflict, now find themselves on the receiving end of Iranian retaliatory attacks. These six nations, all members of the Gulf Cooperation Council, have faced missile and drone strikes targeting both military and civilian infrastructure. Iran’s recent actions stand in stark contrast to previous conflicts where it showed more restraint. These strikes include attacks on airports, ports, and hotels in countries like Qatar, Bahrain, and the UAE, making a clear statement to the Gulf leadership. This marks a departure from Iran’s previous tactics, indicating a deliberate effort to escalate the conflict and create pressure on Gulf nations to urge the US to de-escalate. The Gulf states have tried to mediate peace between Israel and Hamas, as well as between the US and Iran, but with the latest strikes, the region's stability is increasingly at risk. This change in Iran’s tactics is likely to damage the Gulf’s image

item.title: Analyzing Trump's Strategy in Iran: Mixed Messages and Regional Impact
item.description: The article examines the mixed and often contradictory messages coming from the Trump administration regarding its actions in Iran. It questions the reasoning behind America's intervention in the region and raises concerns about the prolonged conflict, especially as millions of people across the Middle East are affected by the ongoing violence. Additionally, the economic impact on Americans is noted, and the article highlights the uncertainty surrounding the administration's goals. The piece also touches on the Clinton family's involvement in the Epstein case, linking it to a broader narrative about political scandals. As the situation in Iran continues to evolve, the future of America's engagement remains unclear, with significant questions surrounding the effectiveness and long-term consequences of Trump's approach. The article ultimately calls into question whether the president's actions in the Middle East are guided by clear policy or shifting political interests.

item.title: Chancellor Rachel Reeves Presents Spring Economic Update in Commons
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves is set to deliver her spring economic statement to the House of Commons, emphasizing that her policies have strengthened the UK's economy. Speaking amid global uncertainty, including tensions in the Middle East, Reeves will highlight the importance of maintaining stability in public finances. The statement is expected to last around 20 minutes and will not include new tax or spending announcements, as the government has committed to holding only one major fiscal event per year. Instead, Reeves will respond to the latest Office for Budget Responsibility forecasts, stressing continued investment in infrastructure and economic reforms. The announcement follows the Bank of England’s recent downgrade of UK GDP growth projections, from 1.2% to 0.9% for 2026 and from 1.6% to 1.5% for 2027. Reeves is expected to point out falling inflation and interest rates as a result of Labour’s prior decisions, asserting that working people across Britain are benefiting. Opposition

item.title: Supreme Court temporarily allows California schools to notify parents about students’ gender transitions
item.description: On March 2, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court issued a ruling allowing California schools to inform parents if their children wish to change their gender identity, temporarily blocking a state law that prohibited automatic parental notification. The emergency appeal was filed by the conservative Thomas More Society, representing two sets of Catholic parents who argued that the law, signed by Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2024, undermined their religious beliefs and parental rights. The parents claimed that the law allowed schools to facilitate gender transitions without their knowledge, violating their right to raise their children according to their religious convictions. California defended the law, emphasizing students’ right to privacy, especially if they feared familial rejection, and argued that state policies aimed to balance student privacy with parental rights. The Supreme Court reinstated a lower-court order blocking the law and related school policies while the case continues. The ruling

item.title: Iranian Women's Soccer Team Stands in Silent Protest During Asian Cup National Anthem
item.description: During the opening match of the 2026 AFC Women's Asian Cup at Cbus Super Stadium in Gold Coast, the Iranian women's national soccer team chose not to sing their national anthem, remaining silent as a form of protest. The players stood stoically as the anthem played before their match against South Korea, where they ultimately lost 3-0. Iran’s manager, Marziyeh Jafari, observed the protest with a smile from the sidelines. This act of silence comes in the wake of heightened tensions in the region following the deaths of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in U.S.-Israeli strikes during Operation Epic Fury, and Iran’s subsequent missile and drone attacks on Israel and allied nations. The team's captain, Zahra Ghanbari, was questioned by media about the situation but the response was cut off, keeping the players’ stance largely unspoken. The broader Iranian football federation has expressed concern about participating in this year’s FIFA World Cup, citing the recent military attac

item.title: U.S. Conducts Precision Strikes on Iran, Congress Informed Under War Powers Resolution
item.description: On February 28, 2026, U.S. forces conducted precision strikes against multiple Iranian targets, including ballistic missile sites, air defenses, maritime mining capabilities, and command-and-control centers, according to a War Powers Resolution letter sent by the White House to Senate President Pro Tempore Chuck Grassley. The strikes were justified as measures to protect U.S. forces in the region, safeguard the free flow of maritime commerce through the Strait of Hormuz, and provide collective self-defense for regional allies, including Israel. The White House emphasized that no U.S. ground forces were deployed and that the operation was designed to minimize civilian casualties while neutralizing Iran's hostile activities. President Trump cited his Commander in Chief authority, not congressional authorization, and indicated that the full scope and duration of future operations remains uncertain, signaling potential escalation. The operation, named 'Operation MIDNIGHT HAMMER,' followed

item.title: Declassified Granted Parliamentary Press Access After Seven-Month Dispute
item.description:
Declassified has secured access to the UK Parliament after a seven-month dispute with Westminster authorities over its application for a press pass. The independent investigative outlet was initially denied accreditation in June, with officials citing limited space. However, internal emails later revealed that decision-makers had referenced the “particular standpoint” of Declassified’s reporting, including its investigations into alleged pro-Israel bias in Westminster. Officials also questioned whether the outlet’s focus on foreign affairs qualified as political coverage.
The rejection sparked criticism from journalists, politicians, and press freedom advocates. Approximately 5,800 readers signed an open letter urging Parliament to reconsider the decision. The letter was backed by more than 100 public figures, including Members of Parliament, prominent journalists, comedians, and leaders of major press freedom organizations. Additionally, 27 MPs from across the political spectrum supp

item.title: AI Coding Startup Cursor Reaches $2 Billion Annualized Revenue
item.description: Cursor, an AI-powered coding assistant founded in 2022, has reportedly surpassed $2 billion in annualized revenue, according to a source cited by Bloomberg. The startup, which initially focused on individual developers, has shifted its strategy toward large corporate clients, who now account for roughly 60% of its revenue. This growth comes despite recent concerns about user attrition, as some developers have switched to competing tools like Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex. Cursor's revenue run rate has doubled over the past three months, demonstrating strong momentum in the AI-assisted software development market. The company was last valued at $29.3 billion following a $2.3 billion funding round co-led by Accel and Coatue in November. Analysts note that while smaller developers are moving away from Cursor for cost reasons, corporate clients tend to remain loyal, providing a stable revenue foundation. Competitors in the sector include Replit, Cognition, and Lovable. Cursor

item.title: Savannah Guthrie and Family Visit Home Following Mother Nancy Guthrie’s Disappearance
item.description: Savannah Guthrie, host of the “Today” show, returned to her mother Nancy Guthrie’s home outside Tucson, Arizona, with her sister Annie Guthrie and brother-in-law Tommaso Cioni on Monday, marking their first visit since Nancy went missing a month ago. The family walked arm-in-arm down the driveway, placing yellow flowers at a makeshift tribute that also featured crosses, prayers, a sign reading “Let Nancy Come Home,” and an angel statuette. Savannah Guthrie later shared a photo of the tribute on social media, expressing gratitude for the support and prayers from neighbors, the Tucson community, and people nationwide, and urged continued hope for her mother’s safe return. Nancy Guthrie, 84, was last seen at her home on January 31 and reported missing the following day.
